short_description: Firewatch is a single-player first-person mystery set in the Wyoming wilderness, where your only emotional lifeline is the person on the other end of a handheld radio.
detailed_description_raw:
Just Updated

Firewatch Patch 03-06-2018

• Fixed a black screen/graphic corruption issue on specific Mac models running High Sierra
• Better performance and threading on all platforms.
• Japanese language subtitles have been added.
• Miscellaneous fixes for all subtitle languages.


About the Game

Firewatch is a single-player first-person mystery set in the Wyoming wilderness.

The year is 1989. You are a man named Henry who has retreated from his messy life to work as a fire lookout in the Wyoming wilderness. Perched high atop a mountain, it’s your job to look for smoke and keep the wilderness safe. An especially hot, dry summer has everyone on edge. Your supervisor Delilah is available to you at all times over a small, handheld radio—your only contact with the world you've left behind. But when something strange draws you out of your lookout tower and into the forest, you’ll explore a wild and unknown environment, facing questions and making choices that can build or destroy the only meaningful relationship you have.

A Note: Firewatch is a video game about adults having adult conversations about adult things. If you plan on playing with a younger gamer, that might be good to know going in.

Includes

  • A stunningly beautiful wilderness environment that expands as you explore.
  • A tailor-made story: the choices you make shape the narrative and build relationships.
  • An edge-of-your-seat mystery.
  • Secrets and discoveries to be made over every hill.

Featuring

  • Living, breathing characters brought to life by Cissy Jones (The Walking Dead: Season 1) and Rich Sommer (Mad Men)
  • A spectacular wilderness environment by Olly Moss (Illustrator) and Jane Ng (The Cave, Brutal Legend)
  • A thrilling story and script by Sean Vanaman and Jake Rodkin (The Walking Dead: Season 1, Poker Night at the Inventory)
  • A stirring original soundtrack by Chris Remo (Gone Home)
  • Fluid first-person animation by James Benson (Ori & The Blind Forest)
  • Gameplay scripting and design work by Patrick Ewing (Twitter) and Nels Anderson (Mark of the Ninja)
  • Programming by Will Armstrong (Bioshock II), Ben Burbank (Costume Quest 2, Space Base DF-9), and Paolo Surricchio (Deadpool, Call of Duty Advanced Warfare)

reviews:
['cinema', 'immersive and emotionally invested story, beautiful graphics', 'this game is just so good', 'Game isn’t loud. It doesn’t try to impress you. It just puts you in the middle of nowhere and lets your mind do the rest. The silence builds tension. Every crack in the forest feels wrong and just when it gets too heavy the radio comes alive. You wait for that voice. Not for guidance, but for comfort. Wyoming’s wilderness isn’t just a backdrop, it’s watching you. So slow down. Look around. Everything here was crafted with purpose.
It’s not about what happens.
It’s about what you feel while nothing happens.', 'Absolute cinema', 'It tells a very adult story about growing up and responsibility.

Much ink has been spilled about the art style, the writing and the delivery. If you are on the fence if you want to spend an evening with a bottle of wine playing this game, just do it.

', 'I went into Firewatch expecting a chill hiking simulator and came out emotionally attached to a disembodied voice on a walkie-talkie. Not sure how that happened, but here we are.

You play as a guy who runs away from his problems… only to realise the forest has excellent acoustics for overthinking. The gameplay is basically: walk, look at pretty trees, climb things that definitely violate workplace safety laws, and make dialogue choices that will haunt you later at 2am.

And the disposable camera you get to snap pics with and then actually see them after you’ve finished, was easily my favourite touch.

Is it action-packed? No.
Will you fight enemies? Also no.
Will you become emotionally compromised over a radio conversation? Unfortunately yes.
', 'This game is a timeless classic that explores grief and guilt and isolation, and what it can do to your psyche. Play it as you truthfully would act in these situations. there are funny, sad, confusing, and beautiful scenes through this whole game. There is so much to explore and i will be playing it again. The story telling is near-perfect', 'Good, anxiety inducing experience', 'This is great fun!', 'xhort but good game, reminds you heavily of the inability to run from reality', "Firewatch is one of the first games I bought on my PC, and after two years I finally decided to play it. I regret not playing it sooner. Firewatch is an amazing game. I'll be honest I don't have a lot of brain power so I can't talk about the message because I don't know it. HOWEVER, I really like the setting and the story and I LOVE the music. The music is incredible and the art style is also incredible and it is just an incredible game. It's a very beautiful game. It was slower than games I'm used to, but I think it was very well-paced. I really like the characters and the interactions between them too.", 'I had this one sitting in my library for a few years. I wasn't sure when I'd "make time" to play it, always postponing it for no real good reason. Likewise, for no good reason, I randomly decided to play the game and was delighted. For the first hour or so, I wasn't sure if this was 'a game for me'. I kept playing and ended up finishing the game in one session and am glad that I did. The game felt uniquely human and easy to relate to. I was able to make decisions that reflected what I myself would do or say in such a situation, which captivated me and kept me hooked till the very end. I was a bit sad when I realized that the game was done, but I am happy that I finally made the time to play it and would recommend others to do the same. Don't think too much about it, just start.', 'Short but sweet.', 'Short but sweet. Everything about this game is so good. From the pacing, to the storytelling, to the end. The only thing about this game is the end was sort of anti climatic, but this game is a good play.', 'Came back after many years and actually finished this game. it has a good story I wish it was a little longer and had some better development. the story was good though i think this is a fun weekend game. took me like 3 hours to play through it all. 6.5/10', 'This game has one of the best stories. The twist at the end was not suspected this game is the best.', 'its okay. i really cant justify the pricetag on this one. voice acting is amazing but the story was weaker than i expected sadly', '8/10', 'txd Di', 'it was good, id loved a couple of diffrent endings thou
', 'What carried it the most was the visuals (and probably the characters too). The story starts off good and the build up got me hooked. But it just falls flat in the end. Left me unsatisfied and made me feel I completely wasted my time. Not to mention, at certain points of the game it just felt too damn tedious. Personally not worth the price, just watch a gameplay of it.', 'Over all a great game and a great story', 'This was amazingly written!', "controller settings are terrible. steam input on or off, it just doesn't match. 
and the game is too short to my liking :(", 'I would have played this for much longer. Loved it.', 'Amazing game with an amazing story, i was hooked played it in all in one 5 hour go, i feel like i felt all the emotions while playing it, sadness, love, was scared at a few moments. 

Would defo recommend it, graphics are also great, and voice acting is soooooo dang good, bummer that the game isnt longer', '🎵"I'm headed off to Ol' Shoshone"🎵', 'Good Storytelling', 'excellent mix of story, visuals, suspense, and problem solving']

short_description: You are the leader of an elite squad of Republic Commandos, your mission is to infiltrate, dominate, and ultimately, annihilate the enemy. Your squad will follow your orders and your lead, working together as a team - instinctively, intelligently, instantly. You are their leader. They are your weapon.
detailed_description_raw:
Chaos has erupted throughout the galaxy. As leader of an elite squad of Republic Commandos, your mission is to infiltrate, dominate, and ultimately, annihilate the enemy. Your squad will follow your orders and your lead, working together as a team - instinctively, intelligently, instantly. You are their leader. They are your weapon.
  • Innovative Squad Control System - With intuitive and smart squad commands, the simple touch of one button easily controls your squad to perform complex commands and strategic maneuvers.
  • Multiple Gaming Mode - Choose the single-player option and command a squad of four that you can dispatch at will. Or, choose the multiplayer option and play with up to sixteen players online in different multi-player modes.
  • Prelude to Episode III - Encounter new vehicles, locations and enemies from the upcoming film.

name: Battlefield 4™
categories: ['Single-player', 'Multi-player', 'PvP', 'Online PvP', 'Steam Achievements', 'Full controller support', 'Steam Trading Cards', 'HDR available']
short_description: Embrace unrivaled destruction in Battlefield 4™. Revel in the glorious chaos of all-out war packed with rewarding, tactical challenges in an interactive environment.
detailed_description_raw:
Battlefield 4™ Premium Edition gives you new maps, modes, and more in one simple package. Complete challenging assignments to unlock new weapons. Dominate tactical challenges in a huge interactive environment — demolish buildings shielding your enemies, lead an assault from the back of a gun boat, or make a little C4 go a long way. In massive 64-player battles, use all your resources and play to your strengths to carve your own path to victory.

Key features

  • Dynamic battlefields — Interactive environments react to your actions in real-time, so you can trigger a shipwreck or flood the streets for a tactical advantage. Your opponents won't know what hit 'em.
  • More vehicles, more freedom — Control the battlefield with dozens of vehicles, from helicopters and stealth jets to gunboats and anti-airs, all designed to give you an edge on massive, chaotic maps.
  • An intense single-player campaign — With international tensions running high, you’ll need to evacuate critical American VIPs from Shanghai and battle against the odds to get your squad home.

Premium Edition includes:

  • All 5 expansion packs — 20 new maps, 48 exclusive assignments, new modes, & more.
  • Exclusive personalization options — Outfit yourself with the latest camos, paints, emblems, and dog tags. With gear for all 4 combat roles, the combinations are endless.
  • Priority position in server queue — Jump into the fight as quickly as possible. Priority position means you can start breaking the enemy faster.
  • 12 bonus Battlepacks — Get your hands on gun attachments, XP boosts, and customizations only available to Premium members.

reviews:
['this is a great fps game frrrr
', 'Battlefield 4 — Campaign Review

This campaign had the bones of a great war story… but never gave itself the time to grow into one.

You follow Recker and his squad through a global conflict that should feel heavy—loyalty, sacrifice, tough calls—but everything moves too fast. The story feels rushed, and the emotional moments don’t land the way they should. Characters like Irish had real potential, but you barely get time to connect before the game pushes you forward.

Where it does deliver is spectacle. Collapsing skyscrapers, violent storms at sea, cities falling apart around you—it looks and feels intense. Classic Battlefield chaos, and it’s done well.

Gameplay is solid but straightforward. Nothing groundbreaking, just reliable shooting and scripted set pieces.

In the end, it’s a one-time ride. Fun in the moment, but not something that sticks with you.', 'better than bf2042, no questions', 'One of the best battlefields that was ever made!!
Excellent gameplay 9.2/10
Superb story 9/10
Fantastic Multiplayer 9.5/10

Very addictive and fun! Highly recommended!
Overall -> 9.3/10', 'the campaign was mid. average shooter campaign but with a plot that is hella stupid and theres hella bugs and glitches. i wish i could even experience the multiplayer but there are no people online so that sucks. and therefore the only reason to pay for this game is for the campaign and thats most definitely not worth ur time. dont buy', 'Graphics still look good, amazing gameplay and decent campaign in my opinion. Multiplayer is still packed too!', 'Top Shooter Game <3', 'idk wat magic they use in 2013 to make dis game', 'the game explains its self', 'My favorite Battlefield game ever. 10/10.', 'Best game ever', 'Amazing game.', "Probably one of the worst battlefields i've played

I only play them for the campaign but oh my god the gunplay felt awful, the campaign was short and generic, with a story that is just uninteresting and unexplained and the ending is just so stupid
I haven't played battlefield 6 and 2042 yet but so far this is the worst one yet", 'Fixing the bug in mission 3, thank you', 'Awesome graphics even in 2026! 

Gameplay is just too good...', 'دينك', 'Yes!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

:)', 'thx good game', 'Battlefield 4 is an all-time classic for me. I pre-ordered it back on console, and even now, it still holds a special place in my library.

Some of my best memories come from jumping into those nonstop servers—24/7 Operation Locker, Golmud Railway, and so many iconic maps that never got old. The gameplay just worked—smooth, chaotic, and incredibly fun in that perfect Battlefield way.

One of the standout features was the massive map-changing events (Levolution)—huge destructible moments that could completely shift the flow of a match. Watching skyscrapers collapse or entire areas transform mid-game made every round feel unpredictable and intense.

Coming back to it, I’m honestly impressed by how well it’s aged. The gunplay, the scale, and those epic Battlefield moments still hit just as hard today.

I genuinely miss the peak days of this game, but it’s still absolutely worth playing now.

10/10 – A true Battlefield classic.', "The Game is dead. Times with well balanced servers long gone.
There's 3 Types of players left:

1: Vehicle ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. 
Cowards that do nothing else than play vehicles. Once they get hit, they either have 3 guys repairing or they drive/fly back to the base and repair. They are the main cause of servers dying. Because 90% of players are either too lazy or too stupid to go against vehicles, so they can just farm and farm and farm... The few remaining servers often have rules with 50% or even less spawn time for vehicles, which makes it even worse.

2: People with zero teamwork, zero awareness and no need at all to capture flags or go against Vehicle ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. I call them GenZ.

3:Movement Hackers.
It's technically not really hacking but what they do is using a glitch, where you throw a medpack in front of you while jumping. If you master this cheating method (which many lvl 140 cowards, bored to play normal anymore do) you're extremely hard to hit, because you just glide through the air as if you have a 500 ping. Makes the movement impossible for foresee to other players.

So yeah, that's it. Would I recommend this game? Back in 2015 until maybe 2017 yes. Nowadays not anymore. 
It got way worse with all the GenZ zombies coming from the failed BF6. But then again, the whole franchise is dead and EA and Dice even braindead. R.I.P.", 'Even after All these Years I have yet to find a better , chaotic shooting game. All though the asians servers are dead , the European servers are full and somehow even with 270 ping its mostly a smooth experience.
', 'great shooting game', 'Very fun as a new player in 2026', 'Still alive, love it', 'Best battlefield all round. Everything is just perfect, the gameplay mix, maps, sound design, sandbox and possibilities, weapon balance and attachment system, progression and mastery. Its just the perfect "modern warfare" battlefield and the cherry on top its a super fckg smooth fps and actually has very interesting movement and general depth', 'по сути я даже заспавнится не успеваю и меня тут же убивают но мне это нравица', 'good game. would be better if we could play with bots in multiplayer since fery few players play this game. im on a really low end pc and get 20 -35 fps but still feels great to play.', "This game was amazing

- Cool shooter
- Solid story

- Recker isn't voiced", 'Vintage BF.', 'fab']

name: Heart of a Warrior
categories: ['Single-player', 'Family Sharing']
short_description: Heart of a warrior is a RPG game in an open world where you play as Nahele, a young warrior of ancient bloodline on his journey to rescue his twin sister from the underworld, as he travel through portals to different realms of existence.
detailed_description_raw:
The 1000 years of the defeat of the dark god, the kingdom of Azputa will have a feast of remembrance to celebrate their victory and their warriors for fought for the realm, but there are those who still worships the dark gods and secretly they planned to open the ancient gates of forbidden unleashing mayhem to the realm of men. The masked ones they are called. But to free the dark god they will need the blood of the old kings whose bloodlines are kept secret.

You will play as Nahele, a young warrior with the old bloodline of kings as he embark on his journey to safe his twin sister from the hands of the dark god. Travelling through portals to different realms to find his way to the dark world.

Quests and Loots
Heart of a warrior is a quest driven adventure game, the main objective is to survive and complete the quest. To survive in this world, you will need to gather as much loots as possible, which you only get when you defeat an enemy or from hidden loot boxes scattered around the world.

Dialogues and Interactions with AI
Interact with the AI's by approaching the said AI. Dialogues with AI are text based and drives the quests.

Skills
The Skills depends on the weapon of choice you choose from the Inventory, The arch sword comes with it unique skill and so are other weapons of choice that you retrieve from the world. There are two types of guns available Long gun(sniper) and Short gun. The long gun is used to target enemies from far distance and the short gun for enemies that are close by.

Combat
Heart of a Warrior features an action-based first person combat system. It is a tab-targeting or auto attacks. In Heart of a Warrior the player’s weapon really matters and a player with better weapon will often defeat a player with less equipment.

Crafting
There is no crafting system available in Heart of a Warrior at the moment but can be included in feature updates. All weapons are already made and available for players to use.

World
Heart of a Warrior is set in the great world of Azupta. The first continent players will enter is called azupta , it is the realm of men and contains the gates of forbidden. The land of the Halflings is the second world the player will encounter and is a massive vegetation containing different types of creatures friend and foe. In total , there are 5 realms to visit (The Realm of men, The world of the halfling, The cursed lands, he desert world and the dark world) all rendered is stunning detail using Unity3d game engine.

reviews:
["Got this game for free and promised to review it fairly so here goes.

This game is rough, first it would not display full screen on my rig (a fairly common one, GTX 1070, 16GB of ram and a R5 3600) no mnatter what resolution I choose. But let's not get ahead of ourselves and list the pros and cons.

- Pros:

It's open world and does not look like to have invisible walls, at least none that I could find
It runs way better than this promotional video
Music is decent so are animations
It asks you what resolution and what screen you want to run the game on when you ;aimch it

- Cons:

short_description: Overcooked returns with a brand-new helping of chaotic cooking action! Journey back to the Onion Kingdom and assemble your team of chefs in classic couch co-op or online play for up to four players. Hold onto your aprons… it’s time to save the world again!
detailed_description_raw:
Overcooked! Version Comparison


About the Game

Overcooked returns with a brand-new helping of chaotic cooking action! Journey back to the Onion Kingdom and assemble your team of chefs in classic couch co-op or online play for up to four players. Hold onto your aprons … it’s time to save the world (again!)

Out of the frying pan, into the fire…

You’ve saved the world from the Ever Peckish. Now a new threat has arisen and it’s time to get back in the kitchen to stave off the hunger of The Unbread!

ONLINE/LOCAL MULTIPLAYER MADNESS

You’ll knead to work together (or against each other) to get the highest score in chaotic local and online multiplayer.

FEAST YOUR EYES ON THIS

Journey across a brand new overworld map by land, sea and air. Get cooking in new themes ranging from sushi restaurants, magic schools, mines and even alien planets!

WHET YOUR APPETITE!

Travel the land cooking up a range of new recipes that are sure to cater to any tastes, including sushi, cakes, burgers and pizzas.

ROMAINE CALM!

Travel through teleporters, across moving platforms and save time by throwing ingredients across dynamic kitchens that shift and evolve. Some kitchens even whisk your chefs away to new locations.

name: Pillars of Eternity

short_description: Prepare to be enchanted by a world where the choices you make and the paths you choose shape your destiny. Obsidian Entertainment, the developer of Fallout: New Vegas™ and South Park: The Stick of Truth™, together with Paradox Interactive is proud to present Pillars of Eternity.
detailed_description_raw:
Prepare to be enchanted by a world where the choices you make and the paths you choose shape your destiny. Obsidian Entertainment, the developer of Fallout: New Vegas™ and South Park: The Stick of Truth™, together with Paradox Interactive is proud to present Pillars of Eternity.

Recapture the deep sense of exploration, the joy of a pulsating adventure, and the thrill of leading your own band of companions across a new fantasy realm and into the depths of monster-infested dungeons in search of lost treasures and ancient mysteries.

So gather your party, venture forth, and embrace adventure as you delve into a realm of wonder, nostalgia, and the excitement of classic RPGs with Obsidian’s Pillars of Eternity!

Play as Any One of Six Races

Human, Aumaua, Dwarf, Elf, Godlike and Orlan.

Utilize Five Core Skills to Overcome Any Situation

Stealth, Athletics, Lore, Mechanics and Survival.

Deep Character Customization

Build a character as one of eleven classes such as Barbarian, Chanter, Cipher, Druid, Fighter, Monk, Paladin, Priest, Ranger, Rogue and Wizard.

Sculpt Your Own Story

Side with various factions using a reputation system, where your actions and choices have far-reaching consequences.

Explore a Rich and Diverse World

Beautiful pre-rendered environments laced with an engaging story and characters bring the world to life.

Play Your Way 

Choose to play with either real-time with pause or turn based mode. You can swap between the two freely during your adventure, giving you full control over every encounter.

reviews:
["Seems like BG3 without all the ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ timed quests that the game doesn't communicate about in any way. Very enjoyable so far", "I really liked the 2nd PoE, but the first one just feels... old maybe? I know it's made to be like one of the old Infinity Engine games, but I'd appreciate some modern upgrades like in Pathfinder. Also, the combat feels very unimpactful. Tried to play it twice, both times gave up.", 'Out of sheer boredom, I played Pillars of Eternity, something I bought from a Steam Sale some years back. It took me a while to gain a grasp of its gameplay, but to keep it short and simple, I'd rather not y'all play it.

Pillars of Eternity is filled with convoluted lore, incomplete voices, questionable choices, and frustrating real-time/pause battle. No, the turn-based mode introduced early April 2026 did not help. You're expected to be a "do-it-all" type of character, making your companions useless conversation fodder.

Healing in battle is not fun because what do you mean a 5th-level healing spell can only heal less than a quarter of a PC's endurance? It would be okay if you can upcast your spells, but no, the lack of strong healing coupled with healing spells' lackluster area of effect make combat a hassle when you're fighting against dragons and other party busters.

Soulbound items add an additional frustration because you get most of them at the end of the game yet they require specific kills WITH the weapon and NOT having the weapon equipped, which is logical in practice but irritatingly stupid when it's a party-based game where two or three characters can decimate enemies in seconds if you let them and let your party die if you choose to turn off their AI.

Then there's the plot and the character endings where if you invest in reasonable-sounding choices two of the characters become suicidal in their own way, with one becoming a thrill seeker and the other just a murderous sociopath. Each faction of Defiance Bay started nuanced and ended useless; the entire storyline of the game is just an exercise in futility.

The only lesson I got from Pillars of Eternity is that beliefs are infallible when it comes to stupid people, and all of the characters are stupid to the point that it wasn't even worth empathizing with its villain despite his rather reasonable but incredibly flawed reasons because beliefs are infallible and everyone is stupid.

I can't get back the 100 hours I played, but I'm hoping that you don't spend a single cent on this game. Just find the game elsewhere.', "Game got off to a rough start... idk just didn't hook me right away story wise, but if you are familiar with the old Pool of Radience game this will scratch that itch. Give it a chance you'll love it.", 'After investing in the Kickstarter, spending 90 hours on it 11 years ago I quit playing it and forgot everything about it.  It has turn based mode now.', 'Fantastic game - Fantastic story, great combat, interesting setting. The game is also thought proving and will keep you thinking a bit afterwards. Loved it!', 'One of the very TOP games in this genre ever.', "Good stuff for the old folks who loved Baldur's Gate 1 & 2", 'Surprised to find I never reviewed this game.  Played this on release for a while before I ditched it.  Only played it as long as I did because nothing else was out.  The stat system in the game is just bad(too much reinventing the wheel) and the RTwP combat system wasn't good in this game....in fact boring...and there was a lot of it.  Trudged through it far too long before trash fight after uninspiring trash fight just got on my nerves.  Tried it again years later and barely made it a few hours before the combat again had me tap out.  Now with turn-based mode, I thought this could perhaps be a salvageable game.  Nope!  Turn-based combat is even worse than RTwP in this game, and wasn't sure that was possible.

First, engagement distance.  No 5ft step here....not even 5 inches.  You can't even rotate around the mob you are engaged in without taking a hit.  Once you engage in melee, you're just stuck there.  Dumb.  Should be able to at least move if you are still right next to the mob.  Next, spell casting.  Not all spells are instant.  So you "cast" a spell and several initiative places later it casts.  Yeah, that's fun when all the mobs have now moved twice so you cast at an empty space.  So you're forced to wait wait wait until there is a gaggle of melee all bunched and engaged before you can cast.  Not sure I've ever seen that in a game before, and don't want to see it again.  Finally, the dual monitor bug.  When you alt tab to another screen, then go back to the game, the cursor is no longer locked to the game screen.  So when you try to mouse to the edge to move the game screen off the cursor goes to your 2nd monitor.  I have to F5/F8 just to fix it.  Annoying.  

Eh, there's more but you get the point.  This game just never was for me.  It's one of the few RPGs I've never finished in more than 4 decades of gaming.', 'Loved it 10 years ago. Loving it now with the addition of turn-based mode.', "Not grate, not terrible. 45/50h was alright that's better, than 20/50h of Original Sin 1. Plus I gained understanding, that prefer European storytelling. The end were suffocating, but at least revealing - Avoved now has much more sense. Probably I'll go and try the second one next.", 'The game is fantastic.

The world of Eora is complex and intriguing, the characters are complex and the dialogue is finger licking good.
Its obvious from the start that the game was designed ground up with RTWP in mind, because it doesnt try to force a turn based system into RTWP as many of the classic CRPGs do, no shade to those. I give the combat system my highest praise as someone who thought they hated RTWP mostly due to Baldurs Gate 1 and the Pathfinder video games.

I played the entire game on the Steamdeck and with some quick key rebinding, the game posed no problems at all, being played with a controller essentially. The haptic surface acting as a mouse makes selecting, boxing or giving orders to units a breeze. However the game does rev the hardware as if you were running the newest unoptimized AAAslop. I could at most play for an hour or so before having to plug in the charger.', 'Amazing game for anyone who likes BG3 or the Beamdog games', '>obsidian strikes aging instance crash on first boot up
>one of the best CRPG i have play 
>main story has soo much depth and twist and soo well written.
>i started playing right when trun based combat update came so i really dont know whats the default combat feels like
>side quest and companion quest are great as well
>obsidian cooked but they forgot to cook outer worlds such a SHAME
>anyways great CRPG', "This is a pretty hard one to recommend just due to the fact that the beginning and pretty much all of act 1 is painfully boring. There's somehow an insane amount of text to read, but also little to no relevant information actually given to flesh out the story. There's also not very much in terms of emotional stakes or hooks to invest you as the player to the story and world. At times it feels less like a story and more like reading a textbook about a reality that doesn't actually exist. Its very... spectrum coded.

That said the combat was a lot of fun to me and the builds are deep and things actually start happening 1/3rd of the way through the game. 6/10 buy on sale only if you've already liked other similar CRPGs otherwise I would say just skip to Deadfire and maybe come back if you really liked it.", 'PEAK OF PEAK', 'I found the game to be a snooze fest and at this point I just want to finish it and get it over with. Its high are okay, its lows are devastating.', 'once a very good game, now it has been updated to be unplayable.

broken AI, teleporting mobs, broken pathing, and CTD. all of then happening multiple time in the 4 or so hrs i have played recently with the new patch. the worst is when one of your partys pathing breaks in turn based combat it causes a soft locked forcing a reload.

TLDR update broke the game =(', "I've always loved both PoE 1 and 2. But I've never written a review for them. But with the release of Turn-based mode, I think it's a good time to say: both games are great. Turn-based just makes them even better, in my opinion, as real-time combat was always my complaint with games like this.", "Adding turn based mode (which is really fun) 11 years after release is crazy. It's been years since I last played and this is still one of my favorite RPGs ever.", 'Really liking the new turn based mode so far. Love that the devs went back and added that new mode to this game.', "It's an amazing game, with interesting story and deep mechanics / character building.", 'Still one of the best-written RPGs I’ve played, but yeah… there’s a lot of reading.

I ended up making a small TTS mod during my playthrough (started as an accessibility thing for my wife), and it actually made a huge difference. Being able to just listen to dialogue, journal text, etc., makes longer sessions way easier.

Great game either way 👍', "One of the best games I've ever played", "Better than Baldur's Gate 1 or 2", "The best ending to a story that I've seen in games. short_description: Outriders’ brutal and bloody combat combines frenetic gunplay, violent powers and deep RPG systems to create a true genre hybrid.
detailed_description_raw:
OUTRIDERS COMPLETE EDITION


OUTRIDERS COMPLETE EDITION includes all the released content for OUTRIDERS:

  • OUTRIDERS
  • WORLDSLAYER Expansion
  • HELL'S RANGERS Content pack
  • Five Exclusive Emotes

OUTRIDERS is a brutal looter-shooter with unique classes, visceral gunplay and powers, set in an original dark universe.
Play both the story and endgame modes in single-player or with up to two friends in seamless drop-in drop-out co-op.


OUTRIDERS WORLDSLAYER EXPANSION



The WORLDSLAYER EXPANSION allows owners of OUTRIDERS to dive straight into the new WORLDSLAYER game content.

With new horrors to challenge you and new weapons, mods and gear to discover, you’ll be further developing your build through the new PAX hybrid branch class trees.

Survive the campaign and you’ll face even greater horrors in the Trials of Tarya Grata, the brand new endgame. Which partnered with Ascension Points offers a new long term progression system for the most committed players.

Finally, push hard through the new Apocalypse difficulty tiers, with each step up to level 40 increasing the chances of obtaining incredible Apocalypse gear and its game changing third mod slot.


Reviews & Accolades

"Brutal and Unrelenting" - MMORPG

Absolutely Incredible” - Gamers Heroes

“THE SCI-FI RPG EPIC WE’VE BEEN WAITING FOR” - COGConnected

“Grisly combo of gunplay and superpowers” - IGN

“A bonafide hit” - Heavy


About the Game


OUTRIDERS is a 1-3 player co-op RPG shooter set in an original, dark and desperate sci-fi universe.

As mankind bleeds out in the trenches of Enoch, you’ll create your own Outrider and embark on a journey across the hostile planet.

With rich storytelling spanning a diverse world, you’ll leave behind the slums and shanty towns of the First City and traverse forests, mountains and desert in the pursuit of a mysterious signal.

Combining intense gunplay with violent powers and an arsenal of increasingly twisted weaponry and gear-sets, OUTRIDERS offers countless hours of gameplay from one of the finest shooter developers in the industry – People Can Fly.

A JOURNEY OF DEATH

As mankind bleeds out in the trenches of Enoch, you must journey across the hostile alien planet in the pursuit of a mysterious signal that could save what’s left of humanity. Should you survive, the powerful Ereshkigal will be your ultimate test.

FOUR UNIQUE CLASSES

Choose from four distinct classes, each with its own unique abilities to define your personal playstyle. Assassinate your enemies as the Trickster, incinerate them as the Pyromancer, punish them up close as the Devastator, or engage them at range as the Technomancer, master of heavy ordinance.

LOOK AND FEEL LEGENDARY

Equip yourself with twisted guns and gear with unique savage sci-fi designs made from heavy, brutal metal and feral organic materials. And transmog your appearance effortlessly to any piece of gear you've collected.

DEADLY BUILDCRAFTING

Discover countless builds to crush your enemies in new and unique ways. Fine-tune your loadout with hundreds of mods across your guns and gear, multiple class-based skill trees, and attribute points. Once your build is perfected, you can turn up the heat by increasing the world tier to face harder enemies and receive better loot.

APOCALYPTIC ENDGAME

Put your skills to the test in Expeditions: 18 hand-crafted endgame levels featuring unique mechanics and challenges. And even greater horrors await in The Trial of Tarya Gratar, an intense dungeon crawl with brutal bosses.

reviews:
['Great concept, clown-factory combat design. No jump, no real melee, and the game expects me to mag-dump a rifle while 10 teleporting crackheads exfoliate my back. Who designed this, a focus group of houseplants?

The last update was in 2024. If the devs do not even care enough to spend time on their own game, imagine me.', "Though recommended requirements state: Intel i7-7700K / AMD Ryzen 5 2600X for a 1080P / 60 FPS experience I'm playing this on a 2009 I7-920 @1080P / 60 FPS.  Admittedly I'm using Lossless Scaling which most think of as a GPU utility though can significantly reduce CPU load as well.   

I acquired the game through a Humble Bundle 1st tier @$10 and given the four really good games in that tier, my mathematical enriched brain calculates my Outriders purchase to a whooping $2.50.   My oh my, how gaming on a budget has become mainstream for those not wanting to rain $60, $70 and $80 on publishers or hundreds, thousands on greedy hardware companies that care little for the little guy / gal when they get in bed with monstrous A.I. conglomerates in order to be entertained.

Once you beat the game it just becomes a dungeon crawler as well.", 'why do people hate it', 'Great game. Hundreds of hours on PS5 and had to pick it up here. Shame its not cross progression but its okay, I love the game.', 'i had a bit of fun', 'This game could not be more mid', "Played the game when it came out on Playstation, back then it was really fun. Then came worldslayer, a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ dlc not worth its price but ehh the game was still fun with friends. Then the major nerfs happened and the support was droped. Now your stuck with a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ annoyance that didnt even come close to the fun it was:

The endgame is terrible boring, a absolute catastrophe in terms of constantly being frozen over and over and over and over by enemy's, the loot is even more horrible now then Diablo 4 at its peak ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ loot season( currently season 12, which is the worst of all)... The lootpool is way to big as is the mod pool that can roll on the loot, i basically spend 20hrs to try and get at least one or two decent pieces of the Shieldbeast set with a usefull 3rd roll, guess what 0 chance and i am flexible in terms of the mod, prolly something about 15ish different variants that could have worked as 3rd mod but forget that. 
Old or new bugs dont get fixed anymore and overall the game is just dead. 

Footnote - even the crossplatform multiplayer DOES NOT FUNCTION at all properly, expect constant disconnects or serverkicks if you want to play pc + console(tried with a friend on xbox, absolutly impossible)

So save your bucks and get something better, its sad really... the game really WAS fun before they decided to completely ruin it.", 'I mean... for just 20 EUR (if without discount) the game is prefect. Satisfying shooting, some kind of plot, always looking for the better gear and the variety of enemies make it really worth it. The game has "The Division" vibes, missions could be repetitive but if this does not bother you - you are gonna like it. Totally worth the price and spent time in my opinion.

P.S. I really prefer singleplayer, so can not tell you anything about coop in the game', 'I cannot recommend this game enough. It is a tragedy that this game will not get a sequel man. 

Firstly, the campaign is awesome. It is a full fledged 25-30 hour story with twists and turns you would not expect from your typical "looter shooter" story mode. PCF put alot into the story and the world building behind this game, this was not half assed, they deff went for something with the tone and humour. I found myself reading almost all of the journal entries because i found the story of the world and the people in it intriguing. 

Not only is the story engaging, but unlike most loot games, the gear game starts from very early in your campaign experience. Awesome loot isnt locked behind endgame, mere hours into the game you start to find powerful mods on loot that make you alter your playstyle. The gear game in Outriders is up there as one of the best in the genre (from my admittedly limited experience). The mods/perks are fun, creative, meaningful and powerful, they encourage experimentation and multiple playthroughs/character rolls. The class system contributes to that as well as there are 4 classes all with about 9 different skills and so many uniqure perks/armour sets tied to those skills. 

The gameplay itself is so fun, it really harkens back to that xbox 360 era shooter power fantasy, channeling the likes of Gears of War in feel. I mean this in the best possible way, it offers a unique experience from that perspective, a modern twist to that very nostalgic game feel. The game isn't gonna crush any graphics cards but i do appreciate it's artstyle, even more so with the World Slayer DLC, as the environments added to the game offer some very beautiful landscapes to traverse. 

Performance on my Legion Laptop with an RTX 4080 was very smooth, no complaints on that side, atleast for the base game. I just started the DLC and all of a sudden i get some hitches and crashes, but so far nothing that ruins my love for the game. 

TLDR: A MUST PLAY FOR LOOT GAME FANS and I would a;so strongly recommend it for gamers who just like playing through shooter campaigns. The story is surprisingly solid.', 'Even with a good graphics card and stuff this game on PC is crap. If you really want to play it well I suggest playing it on console instead hear', 'game is basically unplayable at this point. keep getting disconnected from the server', 'If you like bad b movies, this games storyline humor is for you. All classes are good, solo or Co op. Good game abandon by greedy square enix. Add me if you need help or just want to run a few.']

short_description: The award-winning series returns as Karl Fairburne fights to uncover Project Kraken in 1944 France. The genre-defining authentic sniping, with enhanced kill cam, has never looked or felt better as you fight across immersive maps to stop the Nazi war machine in its tracks.
detailed_description_raw:
Sniper Elite 5: Complete Edition


About the Game



The latest instalment in the award-winning series, Sniper Elite 5 offers unparalleled sniping, tactical third-person combat and an enhanced kill cam. Fight your way across the most immersive maps yet, with many real-world locations captured in stunning detail, and an improved traversal system that lets you explore more of them than ever before.

France, 1944 – As part of a covert US Rangers operation to weaken the Atlantikwall fortifications along the coast of Brittany, elite marksman Karl Fairburne makes contact with the French Resistance. Soon they uncover a secret Nazi project that threatens to end the war before the Allies can even invade Europe: Operation Kraken.



EXPANSIVE CAMPAIGN
Many real-world locations have been captured using photogrammetry to recreate a living, immersive environment, and multiple infiltration and extraction points and kill list targets provide a whole new perspective on each mission. Take on the Nazi plot solo or work with a partner, with improved co-op mechanics allowing you to share ammo and items, give orders and heal each other.



ADVANCED GUNPLAY PHYSICS AND TRAVERSAL
Use ziplines, slide down slopes and shimmy along ledges to reach the perfect vantage point, or to sneak past a sharp-eyed lookout. Factor in rifle stock and barrel options along with gravity, wind and heart rate while you line up your sights on the target.



HIGH CALIBRE CUSTOMISATION
Use workbenches to customise and upgrade virtually every aspect of your weapon – change scopes, stocks, barrels, magazines and more. Rifles, secondaries and pistols all have a huge variety of options. On top of that you can select the ammo to suit your target, from armour-piercing right down to non-lethal.

INVASION MODE – CAMPAIGN DROP IN PvP AND CO-OP
Invade another player’s Campaign as an Axis sniper and engage in a deadly game of cat and mouse, providing a new dimension to the challenge as you stalk your prey. Alternatively, as Karl you can call for assistance and have a second sniper drop in to help you out of a tricky situation.

TENSE ADVERSARIAL MULTIPLAYER
Customise your character and loadout and earn XP, medals and ribbons as you take on intensely competitive 16 player battles that will really test your sharpshooting skills. If co-op’s more your style, you can team up with up to 3 other players against waves of enemies in Survival mode.



ENHANCED KILL CAM
More realistic and grisly than ever, the trademark X-ray kill cam returns, showing you the true destructive power of each shot. Bones deflect bullets unpredictably, ripping a new path through enemy bodies. SMGs and pistols can also trigger kill cams, including multiple shots in dramatic slow motion.

reviews:
['Did anyone on the team behind Sniper Elite 5 actually play their own game?

The movement feels unbelievably stiff — Karl handles like he’s pushing 80 instead of being a trained WWII operative. Reloading a pistol takes what feels like three full seconds. With that kind of “fitness” and reaction time, how is this guy supposed to survive a battlefield?

And the traversal makes even less sense. Why can’t you just drop down from a low wall? We’re talking about something under two meters, and somehow this elite agent is completely stuck unless the game explicitly allows it. It’s clunky, restrictive, and honestly embarrassing for a modern game.

Then there’s the enemy AI. Their patrol and detection logic is absurd — they act like they’ve all got built-in radar in their heads. There’s no nuance, no believable behavior, just instant awareness that kills any sense of stealth or immersion.

And honestly, if you had bothered to replay Sniper Elite 4, the difference would be painfully obvious. Just a few minutes of comparison would show how much worse the handling feels now — it’s genuinely unpleasant.

On top of all that, there are countless little issues that make playing this game feel unnecessarily frustrating — like everything is working against you instead of with you.

Overall, it’s a clunky, awkward experience that feels far more irritating than it should be.', 'You can finally play as the good guys in Axis Invasion Mode', "It is a very good game even if some maps feels the same. 8/10 a notch under Hitman World of Assassination PS : I didn't play any DLCs of Sniper Elite 5 and I didn't play resistance or Sniper Elite 4 but I'll try 4 soon", "Hell, these maps are huge, I just shoot and loot for 3 hours and find out there's like 30%-40% undiscovered area, though I still like them more than SE4's.

Collectible letters in SE4 has better writing. I know, I love to read these letters, they make them human. 

I got with full price, I don't mind it, but def wish I got it with a discount LOL. Anyways, love this game, 100% would recommend.", 'yes', 'Snipe Elite 5', "Gave me 90s and 2000s War game vibes with ALOT of hardware updates sense that era. Love a good shooter with different paths and approaches to missions without too much on the story, where you are the story. Love that. Who don't love a slow-mo kill cam? Def a fun game to play through.", "It's in line with other sniper elite games. Whether you want to be sneaky beaky or go guns blazing, that's completely up to you.", "First off:
Game requires internet to play at all.

Highly detailed visuals for the map, Enemy AI from what I've heard is not as smart as in SE4
In terms of actually Sniping on this game, there is, believe it.. 2 or 3 non-dlc maps that have a good amount of 400+ meter shots when progressing the map normally.
I recommend Iron Sights when sniping, makes it more fun.

and also if I killed you while playing Axis Invader, L + Ratio, I have no scope glint with iron sights, but you do. 45+ kills in under 5 hours of playing axis invader", 'It was good.   not worth full price get it on sale', '', 'I love robust mechanics, I also love wonky jank. This shooter encapsulates both and is sure to deliver, from the diehard fans of the series to the unsuspecting newcomers.', "After having played all the Sniper Elite games (except V2 and Resistance) I have to say that Sniper Elite 5 is by far the best in the series, altough I have absolutely no idea how would it be possible to do Missions in Stealth from Mission 3 and onwards, it is a very entertaining game with bunch of objectives, and enemies. I would say the maps are too big tho for what the objectives are, but it certainly gives countless oportunities to Snipe from, so I'll give them that.

That being said, It does not worth the full price, so get it on a massive sale, because as fun as it is, it can get very repetitive.", "If you have played any of the other games, it's more of the same. Just think of it as having more maps and missions.", "They should make a DLC where you get to assassinate Trump. Apart from that, it's satisfying enough shooting Nazi testicles.", 'Awsome cool gamplay and story is great', '', 'Good Game', 'Best sniper elite game in my humble opinion', "Felt like Hitman and Battlefield, all mixed together, it's very good.", 'Absolutely loved it', "I don't know what to say! I know this game and its theme, and I knew it back in third grade, back when the disc generation was around. I had a lot of memories, but I only knew it was unique among the typical stealth shooters, but with dynamic shooting and a unique storyline. Of course, I liked it not only for its refined design and gameplay, but also for its creativity. And in my opinion, it was one of the best sniper games of its time. Frankly, I wasn't convinced by its positive and more or less appreciated features, but that didn't make it any less appealing. So, I'm not a fan per se, but I was nostalgic and remembered many cool moments from that time", "The best Sniper Elite game. Bar none.

The game play has been well itterated upon, everything you'd want from a Sniper Elite game is here and in its absolute best shape. The guns are good, the ai is smart and the level design is excellent. Years of hardwork and refinement are fully on display here.

Its a geniunely fantastic game that I highly recommend. Even to those who have not played the other Sniper Elites.", 'Buy this game, enough said.', 'Great game!', 'A solid tactical shooter that does exactly what it promises. The sniping mechanics feel satisfying, and the X-ray kill cam never gets old. Missions are well-designed with multiple approaches, encouraging stealth and strategy.

The gameplay loop can feel repetitive after a while, and the story isn’t anything special, but the core gameplay carries the experience.', 'The only way too play this is on Authentic Difficulty. This game is a LOT of fun!', 'shoot nazi much fun', 'My dad and I have been a long time fan of the sniper elite series, from watching him play the first one on our old Hp home computer when I was younger, to playing survival with him with the cross platform in 5. 

Sure, this game has its bugs. In invasion if you shoot the host while they perform a melee kill on you, it can make the invader drop all their weapons and not be able to pick up others. Or how tanks can get stuck pathfinding and keep doing circles in one spot. 

But this game also has great improvements over the last. AI is immersive in the harder difficulties: taking out officers can scatter the infantry and make them act disorganized and panic. Or the skill tree giving players something to work towards, making you and even deadlier sniper!

Overall, the bugs were expected, they happen in every game. Looking past them you can really see just how many improvements Sniper Elite 5 made over the last.', 'Am enjoying the game']

name: Shadow Tactics: Blades of the Shogun

short_description: Shadow Tactics is a hardcore tactical stealth game set in Japan around the Edo period. A new Shogun seizes power over Japan and enforces nationwide peace. In his battle against conspiracy and rebellion, he recruits five specialists with extraordinary skills for assassination, sabotage and espionage.
detailed_description_raw:
Buzz



Join our Discord


Additional content available!


About the Game



Shadow Tactics is a hardcore tactical stealth game set in Japan around the Edo period.

Take control of a team of deadly specialists and sneak in the shadows between dozens of enemies. Choose your approach when infiltrating mighty castles, snowy mountain monasteries or hidden forest camps. Set traps, poison your opponents or completely avoid enemy contact.

The group is composed of very different personalities. Working together as a team seems impossible at first. Yet over the course of many missions, trust is won and friendships are made. The characters develop their own dynamic and each member will have to face their own personal demons.

One of the leaders of this team is Hayato, an agile ninja, who clears the way through his enemies silently, with his sword and shuriken. Samurai Mugen prefers a more powerful approach and can defeat more fiends at one time, but thus also forfeiting flexibility. Aiko is a master of camouflage when she distracts enemies disguised as a Geisha. And the street child Yuki places traps and decoys enemies towards their deadly fate. The mysterious marksman Takuma however, relies on his sniper rifle and takes care of the enemies from a distance.

The player has to carefully evaluate his options in order to master the challenging missions: how will the characters behave as a team? Which one is best equipped for each task? How can they best master the given missions? Come up with your own ingenious tactics to vanquish enemies and complete missions.



  • Play as five completely different characters with their unique skill set
  • Conquer seemingly impossible challenges where you are outnumbered ten to one
  • Find dozens of ways to take out your opponents the way you want
  • Jump from roof to roof and climb large buildings to attack the enemy from above
  • Explore beautiful environments of ancient Japan in the Edo era
  • Choose from three difficulty levels to match your skill

reviews:
['Commandos: Ancient Japanese Edition', "brilliant game! beautiful, well made, great fun. definitely worth giving it a try. a new genre for me at the time, but now, i'm a fan!", 'This is just a masterpiece of stealth tactics in a stunning setting!', 'TERRIBLE GENRE notice i said genre not game the game is actually expertly made but the genre is just not for me as a first time player of the genre even beginner feels like it at times takes a herculean effort to even pass levels. Such a shame since the game was actually fun but alas i cant find it in me to force myself to grit through it anymore.', 'Good', 'yes', "I enjoyed this enough to come back to it. It's a great game, with fun characters and classic story line. Don't miss out on it.", 'So that is where Yuki comes from...', 'Shadow Tactics is an enjoyable game, though one that wore out it’s welcome a little bit in the end.

At it’s core its a commandos style isometric tactical stealth game. That’s a genre I have occasionally dabbled in, playing Commando’s with a friend over a decade ago, but otherwise being aware but not really doing more than dipping my toes into the genre.

Shadow Tactics is another of those games that ended up being purchased on the cheap for £3.50 around 3 and a half years ago, and in an effort to actually cut down my backlog, I have been diving into those games I have piled up. I went into Shadow Tactics knowing very little, other than it had very good reviews on steam, was sent in Japan Edo period, was a stealth game, and it looked interesting.

The game rewarded me pretty well. Presentation wise it’s excellent. It might not be AAAA graphics in blistering RTX on vision, but each map is gorgeous. Though the game is fully in isometric view, there’s so many different details on each level, and a general sense of scale. I even found the cutscenes, while in isometric view again, well animated so to give people in it a greater sense of character. The art style is beautiful, and in some ways the game reminded me in look of a table top model piece.

Sound design seems decent. The voice acting was pretty good, I only heard a little of the English VO as I chose to have the Japanese voices on instead, but both seemed good, and again characters seemed distinctive and well acted.

The game ran well. As a sidenote I did until the half way point have a huge issue with crashing, which I thought was likely because it was a Unity engine game. Turns out that Unity doesn’t seem to like recent intel CPUs, which have problems with many newer games and engines, eg Unreal Engine 5. Using intel XTU to turn down the core performance ration by 3 fixed all crashes, and I can’t hold Intel’s shoddy CPUs against this game. 

Storywise it’s not groundbreaking, but it really held my attention. It’s typical historical Japanese fare, feuding factions, plots and assassins. The main villain of the affair is hidden but very obvious, though I did have it spoiled a bit before this reveal by a walk-through I glanced at, but I wasn’t exactly shocked. A major twist 2/3rds of the way into the game wasn’t spoiled for me, and actually I thought worked really well as an emotional gut punch. The decision that a character makes also feels completely in keeping with the time period and code of morality that would govern historical Japan. The writing for each character is pretty decent, being likeable but again not keeping them too modern, and each was distinctive, so while the main plot of the game is humdrum, the fate of the characters really interested me, as did the general historical atmosphere of the game. I’m a sucker for any historical game that keeps the fantasy elements out and has good atmosphere, and this game delivers.

On the gameplay front, I mostly found it very good. It’s as mentioned, typical commandos fare, control up to 5 different characters with different skills and abilities to complete missions such as stealing items or assisting important officials, while trying to avoid detection. The game has handy tools like vision cones and a shadow mode where you can plan actions and then execute them at the push of a button. The game encourages save scumming with a handy popup when you’ve not saved for more than a minute. While at first I found the odd bit of frustration in getting caught, I quickly found the ability to reload and try again got me through this, and I began to approach each situation like a puzzle, and really found the whole thing enjoyable. I only occasionally glanced at a walkthrough and enjoyed figuring each situation out, using the characters in different combinations. 

Minor negatives, I found controlling the characters in some actions when I needed to be quick to avoid detection, eg moving bodies and getting rid of them, a little clunky. I also felt that the game slightly overstayed it’s welcome. By the final mission I felt that the game was starting to get repetitive. The last mission is nothing new, no spoilers but it’s another castle full of guards with a man to assassinate at the top, with nothing to mix it up. By that point all the characters had been introduced and there were no new abilities, so it felt like treading old ground. I confess I used a walkthrough to cheese through the last level.

I have bought the expansion, Aiko’s choice, but will probably give it a break for a bit before moving on to that so it feels a little fresher. Still at 25 hours Shadow Tactics not a short game, and I didn’t think it got too thin until the very end. I think a part of that is that I’m pretty new to the genre and not a stealthy gamer by nature per say. There is plenty here to encourage repeat playthroughs, with badges to encourage different approaches, like no killing or using certain methods to complete missions.

Overall a very enjoyable little game that has opened up a genre to me that I haven’t really got into before.', 'Great game.  Slow stealth smart game play in the isometric world of Shogun.
Fun.
3-31-26

Very solid game, no bugs, nice graphics and fun mechanics.', 'This was definitely not my type of game, that I usually play, but I enjoyed this game, still without big enthusiasm.

Instead of rushing into fights, the game rewards patience and strategy. Each character has unique abilities — from silent assassinations to distractions and long-range kills — and you’ll constantly be figuring out the perfect way to slip past guards, eliminate targets, and stay completely unnoticed.

[b]What works well:[/b]
• [b]Excellent tactical gameplay:[/b] Every level feels like a puzzle where planning and timing are key.
• [b]Unique characters:[/b] Each member of your team has different skills that open multiple ways to solve missions.
• [b]Beautiful level design:[/b] Japanese castles, snowy villages, and forests look fantastic and are packed with strategic opportunities.
• [b]Shadow Mode mechanic:[/b] Lets you plan multiple actions and execute them simultaneously — incredibly satisfying when it works perfectly.

[b]Some negatives:[/b]
• [b]High difficulty:[/b] Mistakes are punished quickly, so expect lots of trial and error.
• [b]Slow pace:[/b] Players looking for fast action may find the stealth gameplay a bit methodical.
• [b]Occasional repetition:[/b] Some mission objectives feel similar over time.

[u]Overall:[/u]
Shadow Tactics is one of the best modern stealth-strategy games, combining smart level design, interesting characters, and deeply satisfying tactical gameplay. If you enjoy carefully planning the perfect infiltration, it’s incredibly rewarding.

[b]Final verdict:[/b]
A brilliant tactical stealth game with challenging missions and fantastic design — perfect for players who enjoy strategy, patience, and pulling off perfectly timed ninja operations.', 'Good Game', 'Great idea, great graphics. short_description: A deadly virus engulfs the residents of Raccoon City in September of 1998, plunging the city into chaos as flesh eating zombies roam the streets for survivors. An unparalleled adrenaline rush, gripping storyline, and unimaginable horrors await you. Witness the return of Resident Evil 2.
detailed_description_raw:
Deluxe Edition

The Deluxe Edition contains the main game and the Extra DLC Pack which includes the following items:
-Leon Costume: "Arklay Sheriff"
-Leon Costume: "Noir"
-Claire Costume: "Military"
-Claire Costume: "Noir"
-Claire Costume: "Elza Walker"
-Deluxe Weapon: "Samurai Edge - Albert Model"
-"Original Ver." Soundtrack Swap


About the Game

The genre-defining masterpiece Resident Evil 2 returns, completely rebuilt from the ground up for a deeper narrative experience. Using Capcom’s proprietary RE Engine, Resident Evil 2 offers a fresh take on the classic survival horror saga with breathtakingly realistic visuals, heart-pounding immersive audio, a new over-the-shoulder camera, and modernized controls on top of gameplay modes from the original game.

In Resident Evil 2, the classic action, tense exploration, and puzzle solving gameplay that defined the Resident Evil series returns. Players join rookie police officer Leon Kennedy and college student Claire Redfield, who are thrust together by a disastrous outbreak in Raccoon City that transformed its population into deadly zombies. Both Leon and Claire have their own separate playable campaigns, allowing players to see the story from both characters’ perspectives. The fate of these two fan favorite characters is in players hands as they work together to survive and get to the bottom of what is behind the terrifying attack on the city. Will they make it out alive?

  • A spine-chilling reimagining of a horror classic - Based on the original release in 1998, the new game has been completely rebuilt from the ground up for a deeper narrative experience.
  • A whole new perspective – New over-the-shoulder camera mode and modernized control scheme creates a more modern take on the survival horror experience and offers players a trip down memory lane with the original gameplay modes from the 1998 release.
  • Terrifyingly realistic visuals – Built on Capcom’s proprietary RE Engine, Resident Evil 2 delivers breathtakingly photorealistic visuals whilst stunning lighting creates an up-close, intense and atmospheric experience as players roam the corridors of the Raccoon City Police Department (RPD).
  • Face the grotesque hordes – Zombies are brought to life with a horrifyingly realistic wet gore effect as they react in real time taking instant visible damage, making every bullet count.
  • Iconic series defining gameplay – Engage in frenzied combat with enemies, explore dark menacing corridors, solve puzzles to access areas and collect and use items discovered around the environment in a terrifying constant fight for survival.
  • See favorite characters in a whole new light - Join rookie police officer Leon S. Kennedy on his first day in the job and college student Claire Redfield, who is searching for her brother amidst a terrifying zombie epidemic.
  • Step into the rookie shoes of both heroes - Enjoy separately playable campaigns for both Leon and Claire, allowing players to see the story from both characters’ perspective.

name: Transistor
categories: ['Single-player', 'Steam Achievements', 'Full controller support', 'Steam Trading Cards', 'Steam Cloud', 'Remote Play on TV', 'Family Sharing']
short_description: Discover the world of Transistor, a sci-fi-themed action RPG from the creators of Bastion.
detailed_description_raw:
From the creators of Bastion, Transistor is a sci-fi themed action RPG that invites you to wield an extraordinary weapon of unknown origin as you fight through a stunning futuristic city. Transistor seamlessly integrates thoughtful strategic planning into a fast-paced action experience, melding responsive gameplay and rich atmospheric storytelling. During the course of the adventure, you will piece together the Transistor's mysteries as you pursue its former owners.

Key Features:

  • An all-new world from the team that created Bastion

  • Configure the powerful Transistor with thousands of possible Function combinations

  • Action-packed real-time combat fused with a robust strategic planning mode

  • Vibrant hand-painted artwork in full 1080p resolution
  • Original soundtrack changes dynamically as the action unfolds
  • Hours of reactive voiceover create a deep and atmospheric story
  • 'Recursion' option introduces procedural battles after finishing the story
  • Fully customizable controls custom-tailored for PC

definitely one of my top 10 games', '[b]Memory, Music & Tactical Time-Bending – Transistor’s Elegant Combat Puzzle[/b]

[i]A stylish sci-fi action RPG where real-time combat blends with turn-based planning, wrapped in a haunting story and one of the most distinctive combat systems in modern indie games.[/i]

[b]Core Combat & Turn() System[/b]  
Transistor’s defining mechanic is the Turn() system — a hybrid of real-time action and tactical planning. You can pause time, queue up precise movements and abilities, then execute them in a burst of controlled chaos. Once the sequence plays out, you’re forced back into real-time vulnerability while abilities recharge. This push-pull between planning and improvisation creates a rhythm where every fight feels like solving a moving puzzle under pressure. 

[b]Function System & Build Crafting[/b]  
Instead of traditional RPG skill trees, abilities (“Functions”) can be used in three ways: as active skills, modifiers to other skills, or passive upgrades. This means every pickup reshapes your toolkit in multiple layers at once, letting you build wildly different combat styles — from heavy control setups to aggressive burst combos or passive-driven synergy builds. Experimentation is constantly rewarded.

[b]Combat Flow & Decision Making[/b]  
Encounters are compact but layered. Real-time movement matters, but optimal play comes from knowing when to commit to Turn() and how to chain abilities efficiently. There’s a constant tension between safety (planning everything) and risk (reacting on the fly), and mastering that balance is where the depth sits.

[b]World & Progression Structure[/b]  
Exploration is relatively linear, but each area introduces new enemy types and mechanics that gradually expand how you think about combat. Progression is less about stats and more about unlocking new Functions that radically change how you approach encounters.

[b]Presentation & Feedback[/b]  
Every mechanic is reinforced by strong audiovisual design — crisp animations, reactive music, and clear ability telegraphs make even complex combat readable. The soundtrack dynamically shifts with intensity, helping reinforce the emotional and tactical rhythm of fights.

[b]Replayability & System Depth[/b]  
Once completed, the game encourages revisiting builds through its “Recursion” mode, letting you experiment with Function combinations in more challenging scenarios. The depth comes not from content volume, but from how many viable ways there are to approach the same combat systems.

[b]Verdict – 10/10[/b]  
Transistor turns combat into a fluid blend of strategy and action, where planning, timing, and build creativity all intertwine into a uniquely elegant tactical system.', "Do you love the art style and amazing soundtrack of hades but want a more tactical and less frenetic experience?

Horrid bonus he is the only one left from the original story.', "Amazing game, thought the gameplay was clunky at the start, but after a while I truly enjoyed it. And it's clear how it's DNA was carried over to hades series of games. Can't every go wrong with supergaint.", 'Absolutely beautiful piece of art', "At first, it feels like a simple indie game, but the further you progress, the more you start questioning everything around you.

The combat system isn't just about fun—it reflects how you understand and experiment with it. Every ability you choose reshapes your entire playstyle, as if you’re redefining yourself with every battle.

The story doesn’t explain itself directly; Instead, it gives you space to observe, infer, and understand. And when everything finally clicks, it hits much harder than you expect.

Transistor quietly asks its main question: If everything about you could be turned into a “power”...would you still be yourself?

Rating: 8/10", "This is simply a perfect game. It has everything I want : beautiful graphics, interesting storyline, fun and engaging gameplay and a beautiful soundtrack. Everything about this game is fun, from start to finish. I absolutely adore the narrator's voice, and I was hooked on trying different combinations during combat.
I am quite litteraly obsessed with everything and anything Supergiant Games make.
1000% would recommend !", "I originally played Transistor many, many years ago and it became then one of my favorite games of all time. I dare say I prefer it over the Hades games, even if I've spent 10x more playtime in them.

I just deeply love the world and the characters presented in Transistor. We get but a glimpse of them, but maybe the mystery is what makes it so captivating. The camerata, flawed but well meaning in their ambitions, make for very compelling antagonists with a grand purpose that crumbled due to very human mistakes. [spoiler] I can't lie, I also really like the queerness of most if not all of their members, although I can't say I'm a huge fan of all the queer people being antagonists in a story predominantly about a straight romance, but still. [/spoiler]

The gameplay is also a huge factor. I played this when I held a deep fascination for game design and spent months dissecting and analysing what made me enjoy the mechanics of Transistor. I don't have the vocabulary or breath of thought to express those ideas anymore sadly, but, I'd like to comment that the versatility they achieved using a very limited number of player actions by allowing us to combine them is impressive. It's also very fun to learn more about the world and its characters by trying new builds and not just defaulting to the ones we most like immediately.

This is a very special game to me and I'm glad I took the time to go through it again, with the achievements and all this time around. I can't wait to see what Supergiant makes next now that Hades might be over, I loved it but I really wish to see a whole new setting that may very well be as fascinating as Cloudbank.", 'A Stylish Action Combat Experience That Bares Its Soul Through Art and Song

Overall Impression
This game is somewhat a hidden gem that can be easily missed. It’s an earlier title in Supergiant’s string of exceptional games. You can see how this title would eventually give way to massive hits such as Hades. Transistor is an action adventure starring Red and her great sword like weapon set in a sci-fi universe where you battle robotic enemies. It has a unique combat system and painterly stylized art combined with an evocative soundscape and environment that pulls you into its poetic world.

Target Audience
This is a game that requires some patience to learn its combat system. This alone may have people drop it early without discovering the hidden gift it offers. There’s actually much more going on with the combat mechanics and skills than you would initially think. There’s a clever combination of planned actions (with a turned system) and active attacks. You don’t see a mix like this often in gaming. Hence those that will really connect with the game will require some patience to learn its systems. For those that do there’s a very stylish action combat experience.
 
Story
In Transistor, you play as Red. She is a singer in a futuristic sci-fi cityscape of Cloudbank. The opening sequences show Red by the body of a man who has been killed by a weapon, named the Transistor. Red’s voice is absorbed by the sword along with the consciousness of the man. The voice of the man through the blade communicates with Red and also acts as a narrator throughout your journey. As enemies are defeated, their data can also be absorbed into the weapon opening up new abilities for the player. The narrative has a unique feel to story telling with the role of the narrator being so prominent. It adds a layer of personality to the experience where you do feel like you are going through the journey as a member of the team. Story elements unfold through each section of the city as well as in cutscenes and in some written texts throughout the experience.

Gameplay
The core combat experience is made up of 2 components. There’s the action combat side where you setup various skills and use them when necessary. Then there’s the planned action combat. This is where you can pause the action and input a string of commands and have them executed all at once. After this planned chain of actions, there is a reset period where you won’t have access to certain abilities. This is where things can get a bit complicated. During the reset period, if you modify your skills appropriately you can still use certain abilities thereby allowing you to constantly be able to attack. This makes it so that in combat you’ll be alternating between planned and live real time attacks.

Adding to the complexity is that skills play multiple roles. Skills can be equipped as an ability but they can also be used to modify other skills. This mixing and matching allows you to customize your skill set. From this setup, you can already see the layers of complexity and depth that will take some time to learn and experiment with.

Another interesting aspect of combat are the skills themselves. Although Red utilizes the Transistor, her abilities are more diverse than what you would expect from a melee type weapon. Throughout the journey Red can acquire various skills. Some of these  include the ability to  shoot and bounce projectiles, cause clusters of explosions and stun enemies. As discussed earlier, each of these skills can be further augmented to produce different effects depending on the modifier that is added. Altogether this gives a pretty diverse range of abilities that are at the player’s disposal.

Art Design & Music
One of the defining characteristics of Supergiant’s games is the art direction. This is no different in Transistor. There’s a beautiful and imaginative painterly aesthetic to the overall look. The characters and the world are rendered in wonderful and vibrant hues that pull you into this imaginative universe. The characters and the surroundings are unique, charming and memorable.

The music is another core component that draws you in. The soundtrack for Transistor could be an album on its own. It’s a beautiful anthem for the character’s journey through this world filled with challenges and hope. Through song you feel the emotion and soul of the protagonist. It forms a powerful connection that only music can evoke. Moreover, the voice acting is also equally impressive. The overall art direction, music and voice acting display much care and dedication to true artistry and craftsmanship. This is a defining quality of the studio and why so many fans are drawn to their games.

Challenges
The main challenge of the game is understanding its core combat system. Most modern games have refined their on-boarding process to introduce elements to a player that are natural, intuitive and not overwhelming. The combat system here is pretty unique as you don’t often see a combination of turn based action planning and real time combat. Along with the option for skills to be used as abilities or modifiers,  and there’s an interesting depth and customization that is available. All this means that it will take some time especially in the early play-through to slow down and understand how these systems work.

Another aspect that can be challenging is how the narrative is told. Although the voice acting is incredible it can be challenging to follow the story. The narrator is intriguing but a lot of the context of what is actually happening here isn’t always explained. Furthermore, with the player trying to learning all the mechanics in the early game can add to some challenges following the story in the early parts of the game.

Closing Thoughts
Transistor is a unique action game with both an active and turn based combat system. Exceptional art direction, music and voice acting are standout features of the title that have long defined Supergiant games. Here you can see much of the building blocks that would eventually lead to other popular titles in their catalgoue. The on-boarding process to understand the system is a bit rough but those who are willing to go through the journey will experience a unique game with a lot of heart and a beautiful soundtrack.
