', "personally i would say it's actually a good game, just at the begginig it's not acually very intuituive 4 new players. rather than that, i like it", 'Tough game, eye hand coordination required, could use with a little extra explanation but I guess it would ruin the theme of being a detective and figuring out your next move on your won.  Watch the cues it gives is best advice I can give.', 'I finished the game on easy mode, but I enjoyed the story it told. I liked the atmosphere of Victorian London.', 'Gameplay was pretty fun and innovative for the series. The puzzles were manageable. My only gripe is I liked the writing/story in Crimes & Punishments better.', 'nice', 'pros

✅ gorgeous, polished graphics and level design featuring Victorian London districts and places like Whitechapel and Highgate Cemetery
✅ there are some fun to play action and stealth sequences
✅ well made cutscenes
✅ there are nice variety and many complex, challenging puzzles.
✅a well-written, engaging, and intriguing main story with some twists and turns and five different and independent criminal cases. The main story is about Sherlock Holmes's stepdaughter, whose criminal father killed the previous game (The Testament of Sherlock Holmes), and he is keeping her true origin as a secret. However, the situation changes when a mysterious woman known as "Alice" becomes Holmes's new neighbor. Alice slowly befriends Katelyn, eventually earning the daughter's trust while causing emotional issues between Holmes and Katelyn at the same time. Throughout the game, Holmes will solve different cases while getting to know Alice's origin and her true intention
✅ It's enjoyable to piece together deductions in Sherlock's thinking
✅ character models and cutscenes looks decent
✅ decent length
✅ decent voice acting
✅ there are steam achievements and cards
✅ there is an map for fast travel

cons

❌ frequent and long loading screens
❌ repetitive gameplay with the same mini-games and scripted sequences
❌ relies too heavily on scripted action and stealth sequences, tedious and frustrating QTEs, and mini-games. Fortunately, you can skip these gameplay elements if you prefer not to engage with them
❌ The Mayan temple puzzle chamber with all these annoying and frustrating traps is tedious to play

Overall, this is an enjoyable addition to the long-running Sherlock Holmes adventure series with multiple engaging storylines, new action and stealth gameplay elements, and well-designed environments.', "Wonderful, movement a little janky but the puzzles and qte's are cool.", 'I'll recommend this out of respect to the developers and due to some clever and fun puzzles, but frankly, this isn't a great game in my opinion.
Buy this on a big sale if you can.

- The title implies that there's a main continuous plot and investigation, but this is another "chapters" game of five different unrelated cases that their conclusion doesn't affect the rest of the game at all. I think it creates weaker immersion.
- At the end of each case you've gathered enough clues to accuse different suspects based on your deductions. This could have been a great game mechanism, but in some cases it really feels that more than one could be true (or even that all of them have a flaw and are illogical). So you choose something and then the game tells you if you were right or wrong but without any reasoning. It doesn't create a sense of achievement or even a sense of failure. Just a sense of arbitrarity which is really bad for a detective game.
- The voice acting of too many characters isn't good. The girl was especially annoying.', "I liked it. Not outstandingly. But it's a good detective game. The deductions both make sense, and are sufficiently hard. The overarching story is serviceable. The puzzles could have been harder. But alas, this is a 2016 Xbox game.

As for how it compares to the games before this, they changed Sherlock! I do still like the new one though. The loss of old Watson is what's more tragic. The new one doesn't act like enough of a foil. I feel he goes along with Sherlock's antics way too easily. Also underutilised.", 'berotak senku', '[h1]Sherlock Holmes: The Devil’s Daughter[/h1]

The heart of the game is investigative gameplay. You poke around crime scenes, gather evidence, deduce motives, and decide the fate of characters — all while occasionally getting dragged into mini-game shenanigans that feel like Sherlock accidentally walked into a carnival.

[b]What works well:[/b]
• Detective vibes: Investigating crimes, analyzing evidence, and connecting the dots makes you feel like a true genius sleuth.
• Choices matter: Your moral decisions can drastically change the outcome of cases — innocent or guilty, it’s all on you.
• Atmosphere: Foggy London, candlelit rooms, and dramatic music set the perfect moody detective scene.
• Variety: Investigations, interrogations, stealth moments, and puzzles keep the gameplay from feeling stale.

[b]Some negatives:[/b]
• Puzzle inconsistency: Some cases make you feel like Sherlock reborn, others like you need a PhD in “random nonsense.”
• Clunky controls: Walking around and interacting with clues can feel awkward at times.
• Mini-games: A mixed bag — some are fun, others are “why am I pressing buttons right now?” frustrating.
• Story pacing: Certain plot twists and endings can feel rushed or undercooked.

[u]Overall:[/u]
Devil’s Daughter is a moody, stylish detective game that scratches the Sherlock itch while giving you control over who lives, who dies, and who’s totally innocent… until you decide otherwise. It’s a smart, engaging adventure with a few rough edges, but when the clues click, it’s genuinely satisfying.

[b]Final verdict:[/b]
A story-driven detective adventure that nails the atmosphere and gives you meaningful choices — imperfect in spots, but still a must-play for mystery lovers and anyone who’s ever wanted to feel like a clever detective in foggy London.', "My second Sherlock Holmes game after Crimes and Punishment. Somehow it is both better and worse than C&P. Graphically it is a massive improvement. Also, the quick time events are more fun. There is more variation in the game making it feel like a more complete game. However, this also leads the game astray from it's core. Sections of the game (no spoilers) feel like I'm playing Indiana Jones. 

Unfortunately I had a lot of technical issues with the game. Frequent crashes in the deduction screen. I was able to fix them using Gemini. After changing some settings I was able to finish the game, but it's not really acceptable that the game is unplayable at times.

All in all, it's a decent Sherlock Holmes game. It began better than C&P but in the end I feel that C&P was a better Sherlock Holmes game. I'd rate this one about a 6/10. If the technical issues were fixed, it would be a 7/10.", 'nice game
', 'Thought I would be solving mysteries, not playing a stupid stealth game']

name: Watch_Dogs™
categories: ['Single-player', 'Multi-player', 'Steam Achievements', 'Steam Trading Cards', 'Partial Controller Support']
short_description: In today's hyper-connected world, Chicago operates under ctOS, the most advanced computer network in America.
detailed_description_raw:
WATCH_DOGS™ COMPLETE EDITION


Experience Watch_Dogs - the phenomenon of 2014 - with the biggest edition ever!
As Aiden Pearce, a brilliant hacker, turn Chicago into the ultimate weapon in your quest for revenge.
In this Complete Edition, including the game + Season Pass, access a new game mode, weapons, outfits and missions and plays as T-Bone, the legendary hacker in a brand new campaign!


About the Game

All it takes is the swipe of a finger. We connect with friends. We buy the latest gadgets and gear. We find out what’s happening in the world. But with that same simple swipe, we cast an increasingly expansive shadow. With each connection, we leave a digital trail that tracks our every move and milestone, our every like and dislike. And it’s not just people. Today, all major cities are networked. Urban infrastructures are monitored and controlled by complex operating systems.

In Watch_Dogs, this system is called the Central Operating System (CTOS) – and it controls almost every piece of the city’s technology and holds key information on all of the city’s residents.

You play as Aiden Pearce, a brilliant hacker and former thug, whose criminal past led to a violent family tragedy. Now on the hunt for those who hurt your family, you'll be able to monitor and hack all who surround you by manipulating everything connected to the city’s network. Access omnipresent security cameras, download personal information to locate a target, control traffic lights and public transportation to stop the enemy…and more.

Use the city of Chicago as your ultimate weapon and exact your own style of revenge.

KEY FEATURES


HACK THE CITY
Watch_Dogs takes place in a fully simulated living city where, using your smartphone, you have real-time control over the city’s infrastructure. Trap your enemy in a 30-car pileup by manipulating the traffic lights. Stop a train, and then board it to evade the authorities. Narrowly escape capture by quickly raising a drawbridge. Anything connected to the city’s CTOS can become your weapon.

STREET JUSTICE
Living in inner city Chicago where violence is best answered by violence, you have the skills to take the fight to the streets. Take down enemies by delivering devastating blows with your baton, or experience a shoot-out like never before with a physics simulation that rivals anything in the shooter category. Use a combination of shooting and hacking to gain advantage in any given situation. The streets are designed for you to create your own plan of attack. Overall, you will have access to more than 30 traditional weapons.

HIGH OCTANE DRIVING
Ubisoft Montreal has partnered with studios such as Ubisoft Reflections, the acclaimed studio behind the award-winning Driver series to bring you a game bursting with horsepower. Get behind the wheel of more than 65 vehicles, each with state of the art physics and handling, and explore the massive city while completing missions.

EVERYTHING IS UNDER CONTROL
Disrupt, the all-new game engine dedicated to Watch_Dogs, uses advanced processing and graphics to deliver a stunning visual and an incredibly realistic experience. Disrupt gives you the power to affect the story, the city and the life of the population with your choices creating a ripple effect throughout the city.

DYNAMIC NAVIGATION
Watch_Dogs gives you the ability to not only use the city’s ctOS to your advantage, but the streets as well. In real-world Chicago, cut through buildings or climb to the rooftops to catch your target.

SEAMLESS MULTIPLAYER EXPERIENCE
Discover new levels of interaction, cooperation and confrontation between players thanks to a brand new multiplayer game system that links the single and multiplayer modes into one seamless experience. That means no menus, no loading screens, just instant open world multiplayer action.

name: The Expendabros
categories: ['Single-player', 'Multi-player', 'Shared/Split Screen', 'Full controller support', 'Remote Play on Phone', 'Remote Play on Tablet', 'Remote Play on TV', 'Remote Play Together']
short_description: The Expendabros have assembled and set their sights on the forces of ruthless arms dealer Conrad Stonebanks in the forests of Eastern Europe.
detailed_description_raw:
The Expendabros have assembled and set their sights on the forces of ruthless arms dealer Conrad Stonebanks in the forests of Eastern Europe. Battle in a flurry of bullets and non-stop action through ten full-throttle missions set amongst the picturesque tree tops and the absurdly dangerous lumber mills teeming with enemy soldiers, excessive artillery, and inconveniently placed circular saws. Rescue your brothers in arms and play as seven different legendary soldiers – each with their own unique weapons and special attacks – with up to four players at once in local multiplayer co-op mode.

The Cast

Bro Caesar | Bro Christmas | Broney Ross | Trent Broser | Bronar Jenson | Broc | Toll Broad

About Broforce

When evil threatens the world, the world calls on Broforce - an under-funded, over-powered paramilitary organization dealing exclusively in excessive force. Brace your loins with up to four players to run ‘n’ gun as dozens of different bros and eliminate the opposing terrorist forces that threaten our way of life. Unleash scores of unique weapons and set off incredible chain reactions of fire, napalm, and limbs in the name of freedom.

About The Expendables 3

In THE EXPENDABLES 3, Barney (Stallone), Christmas (Statham) and the rest of the team come face-to-face with Conrad Stonebanks (Gibson), who years ago co-founded The Expendables with Barney. Stonebanks subsequently became a ruthless arms trader and someone who Barney was forced to kill… or so he thought. Stonebanks, who eluded death once before, now is making it his mission to end The Expendables -- but Barney has other plans. Barney decides that he has to fight old blood with new blood, and brings in a new era of Expendables team members, recruiting individuals who are younger, faster and more tech-savvy. The latest mission becomes a clash of classic old-school style versus high-tech expertise in the Expendables’ most personal battle yet.

Lionsgate and Millennium Films present a Nu Image production. PG-13 for violence including intense sustained gun battles and fight scenes, and for language.

reviews:
['Why do I have 19 hours in this short game?', "Having finished Broforce right before jumping into this, I found both games to be incredibly enjoyable experiences. The highlight of the game is definitely the discovery process; finding each new character and experimenting with their unique abilities is so fun.

If you’ve finished Broforce and find yourself craving more of that signature action, this is absolutely worth your time. While it might just be wishful thinking, I truly hope the developers release a Broforce 2 someday. Honestly, the community would be thrilled even if a sequel kept the exact same mechanics but simply introduced new characters and fresh levels. The core gameplay is just that thrilling.

This is a 10/10 game, and I feel exactly the same way about its Broforce.

At the end of the day, it's a must-play for anyone who loves pixel-art mayhem.

- M ♡", 'The Expendabros: The Ultimate Prequel to the Bro-pocalypse

 If you want to see where the pure, unadulterated chaos of the Broforce formula really proved itself, you have to play The Expendabros. It’s not just a promotional tie-in; it’s a brilliant standalone experience that arguably set the stage for the full game’s success.

 What Makes It a Must-Play:
The OG "Bro" Energy: Even though it came out before the full Broforce release, the gameplay is already pitch-perfect. It’s fast, incredibly smooth, and carries that "just one more level" brilliance.

 Fantastic Character Roster: You get seven legendary soldiers based on the Expendables 3 cast. Seeing these icons "bro-ified" with unique weapons and special moves is a total blast for any action movie fan.  

 The Best Price Tag (Free!): It’s a shorter campaign with 10 missions, but considering it’s completely free, the value is unbeatable. It’s the perfect high-intensity snack for your Steam library.

 Better With Bros: While you can go solo, this game truly shines in local co-op. Grabbing one to three friends for a 4-player destruction derby is some of the most fun you can have on a couch.

It’s short, it’s loud, and it’s a masterpiece of 2D action. name: Call of Duty® 4: Modern Warfare® (2007)
categories: ['Single-player', 'Multi-player', 'Family Sharing']
short_description: The new action-thriller from the award-winning team at Infinity Ward, the creators of the Call of Duty® series, delivers the most intense and cinematic action experience ever. Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare arms gamers with an arsenal of advanced and powerful modern day firepower and transports them to the most treacherous hotspots...
detailed_description_raw:
The new action-thriller from the award-winning team at Infinity Ward, the creators of the Call of Duty® series, delivers the most intense and cinematic action experience ever. Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare arms gamers with an arsenal of advanced and powerful modern day firepower and transports them to the most treacherous hotspots around the globe to take on a rogue enemy group threatening the world.
As both a U.S Marine and British S.A.S. soldier fighting through an unfolding story full of twists and turns, players use sophisticated technology, superior firepower and coordinated land and air strikes on a battlefield where speed, accuracy and communication are essential to victory. The epic title also delivers an added depth of multiplayer action providing online fans an all-new community of persistence, addictive and customizable gameplay.

  • Authentic Advanced Weaponry - Featuring an available arsenal of more than 70 new and authentic weapons and gear from assault rifles with laser sites, claymore mines, .50 caliber sniper rifles, and M-249 SAW machine guns. With accessories like night-vision goggles and ghillie suits, for maximum concealment, Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare has players locked and loaded to accomplish the mission.
  • Coordinated Assault and Support - Delivering the most visceral action thriller ever, the title covers modern battle from the soldier to the satellite, where the need for air support is critical to success. The adrenaline rush deployment enlists gamers to fast-rope from tactical helicopters, ride in an armada of attack choppers, utilize jets to remove enemy strongholds and even engage hostiles from thousands of feet above the ground inside a state of the art aerial gunship.
  • Cinematic Quality Graphics and Sound - Featuring stunning next-generation graphics, players will be drawn into the cinematic intensity of Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare. Amazing special effects, including realistic depth of field, rim-lighting, character self-shadowing, texture streaming as well as physics-enabled effects will enlist players into the most photo-realistic gaming experience. Combine the lifelike graphics and the realistic battle chatter with the Call of Duty award-winning sound design and players will face battle as they have never before.
  • Unparalleled Depth to Multiplayer - Multiplayer builds from the success of Call of Duty 2 delivering a persistent online experience for greater community interaction. Featuring create-a-class options allowing players to customize gear that is best suited for play, to experience points enabling unlockables and perks, all the way to matchmaking and leaderboards for the latest in tracking, Call of Duty 4: Modern Warfare's multiplayer is set to deliver easily accessible and addictive online play for all.

short_description: Codename CURE is a first person, co-operative zombie game with support for up to five players, providing dynamic, fast-paced zombie fragging with both objective, and survival-based missions.
detailed_description_raw:
Workshop

CURE now includes Steam Workshop support


About the Game

Codename CURE is a first person, co-operative zombie game with support for up to five players, providing dynamic, fast-paced zombie fragging with both objective, and survival-based missions. In objective mode play as a team of special military operatives, tasked with “curing” infected areas of a post-apocalyptic world, by planting controlled explosives before pulling a short sharp exit. Pick your speciality out of five player classes: Pointman, Support, Assault, Technician and Sniper, using each ones unique play style and equipment to support your team, and successfully complete each mission. Survival mode is a wave based no-win scenario, test your skills with the odds increasingly stacked against you. Resupply in-between waves with a dynamic selection of weapons and equipment for your best chance to survive until the next round. CURE can be played both on and offline, with or without computer controlled team mates. The game sports a variety of difficulty options which adapt dynamically with the number of players.

short_description: Get Batman: Arkham City and all DLC for one low price with the release of the GOTY Edition!
detailed_description_raw:
Batman: Arkham City builds upon the intense, atmospheric foundation of Batman: Arkham Asylum, sending players flying through the expansive Arkham City - five times larger than the game world in Batman: Arkham Asylum - the new maximum security "home" for all of Gotham City's thugs, gangsters and insane criminal masterminds. Featuring an incredible Rogues Gallery of Gotham City's most dangerous criminals including Catwoman, The Joker, The Riddler, Two-Face, Harley Quinn, The Penguin, Mr. Freeze and many others, the game allows players to genuinely experience what it feels like to be The Dark Knight delivering justice on the streets of Gotham City.

Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition includes the following DLC:

  • Catwoman Pack

  • Nightwing Bundle Pack

  • Robin Bundle Pack

  • Harley Quinn’s Revenge

  • Challenge Map Pack

  • Arkham City Skins Pack

Batman: Arkham City - Game of the Year Edition packages new gameplay content, seven maps, three playable characters, and 12 skins beyond the original retail release:

  • Maps: Wayne Manor, Main Hall, Freight Train, Black Mask, The Joker's Carnival, Iceberg Lounge, and Batcave

  • Playable Characters: Catwoman, Robin and Nightwing

  • Skins: 1970s Batsuit, Year One Batman, The Dark Knight Returns, Earth One Batman, Batman Beyond Batman, Animated Batman, Sinestro Corps Batman, Long Halloween Catwoman, Animated Catwoman, Animated Robin, Red Robin and Animated Nightwing

reviews:
['not as good as arkham asylum story wise, has better graphics and a few more mechanics but not much, one of the main issues i have is that you dont really play as robin but catwoman has a bigger part when in my opinion the roles should be reversed.', 'best game', 'amazing series', 'After completing Batman: Arkham Asylum, which felt contained and intense, Arkham City opens things up in a way that instantly feels iconic.

What really stands out right from the start is the feeling of being Batman. The moment you’re gliding over rooftops, it echoes the dark, grounded tone of The Dark Knight that sense of being a silent guardian over a city that never really sleeps.

What makes Arkham City special isn’t complexity or scale alone, it’s how smoothly everything comes together. Movement, combat, and atmosphere all align to create that one simple feeling "this is what Batman is supposed to feel like". Arkham Asylum made you feel like you were inside a Batman story. But Arkham City makes you feel like you are Batman.

Arkham Asylum laid the foundation, no doubt. But Arkham City is where that foundation turns into something memorable something that sticks with you long after you close the game.', 'Fun!! Can play in my laptop too!', '배트맨이 죠셴징들이나 좀 패줬으면 좋겠네', 'I AM BATMAN', "This game plays so good! I just came from Asylum to the City, and man, it's so cool seeing how developers improved on the combat and fluidity of systems compared to the first (Asylum)! I definitely recommend!!!", 'Better story
Better atmosphere
Better mechanics
Basically took all the great things from asylum and made them better
It’s game of the year for a reason, 10/10', "Greatest superhero game oat. The story was immaculate. Batman's poisoning while still fighting it was something of a miracle. He cured himself and solved the issue of protocol 10. The game was truly a spectacle.", 'such a great game!', "Story is pretty weak imo. It feels much more like a general Batman tribute instead of a proper storyline about Arkham City, you know, the place the entire game is takes place in? Instead it's a hunt for a virus cure and Batman has to encounter several famous villains one after another. Gameplay is much better compared to the last, boss fights are arguably the best in the series. Everyone talks about Mister Freeze for a reason. But there is too much content in the Challenge Mode.", 'riddler can die in a fire.', 'batman', 'PEAK GAME', '10/10. BEYOND EXPECTATIONS.', 'worst open world BY FAR, absolutely repetitive gameplay, godawful plot that turns a corner every ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ minute, but hey the ending is pretty good.', '.', "We already know that Batman Arkham Asylum is very good. This one here, Batman Arkham City (the successor), is EVEN BETTER. I'm having so much fun!", 'Very Good: 8/10', 'This game is a living proof that sequels can be bigger and better if done right!
Compared to Arkham Asylum:
-The world is bigger and looks amazing
-The side missions are many more
-The combat is more responsive and satisfying to master
-The Riddler puzzles are now actual puzzles, to try and twist your mind a little
-The addition of Catwoman and her unique abilities and traversal methods
The only downside is a little the story, because its a bit less captivating than the Asylum one, and the time playing Catwoman feels pretty short, but nonetheless, this was an amazing sequel and worth playing!', 'i play the game its always crash when i want to fight penguin', 'Hugo has some strange plans', 'Batman 2', '8/10', 'at least a 9/10. My favorite superheroes game, so close to the comics with a depth of its own.', 'The pinnacle iteration of The Batman fantasy brought to life, a densly packed 8-10 hour rollercoaster set in a place with so much detail, so much happening within, around, and behind its narrative, gameplay and world. Arkham city is the peak of Arkham and is a masterpiece in its own right.', '10/10 Everything that made Arkham Asylum great, expanded upon. Memorable villain encounters and boss fights. A Masterpiece.', 'probably the best story wise from the three games, fantastic way to start the open world concept for Arkham Knight. short_description: Embrace mind-blowing moments as you’re pulled deep into the many worlds of Split Fiction, a boundary-pushing co-op adventure.
detailed_description_raw:
CRITICALLY ACCLAIMED



About the Game

Embrace mind-blowing moments as you’re pulled deep into the many worlds of Split Fiction, a boundary-pushing co-op action adventure from the studio behind 2021 Game of the Year Winner, It Takes Two. Mio and Zoe are contrasting writers – one writes sci-fi and the other writes fantasy – who become trapped in their own stories after being hooked up to a machine designed to steal their creative ideas. They’ll have to rely on each other to break free with their memories in-tact, working together to master a variety of abilities and overcome diverse challenges while jumping between sci-fi and fantasy worlds in this unexpected tale of friendship.

Split Fiction is a unique action-adventure experience that keeps you on the edge of your couch with unexpected moments. One minute you’re taming adorable dragons and the next you’re fighting as cyber ninjas, escaping terrifying trolls, or dodging hover cars thrown by a robotic parking attendant. It’s weird, it’s wild, and it’s designed to be shared.

Grab your co-op partner and get ready to overcome any obstacle thrown your way.

everything’s twice as fun when you play together', 'I like it', 'good
', 'Awesome game about stories that is itself a collection of minigames! Perfect to play with a partner or friend!', 'The first couple of hours are super rough. The overarching story is underwhelming and the protagonists don’t land at all, which makes for a very poor first impression.

But as you dive deeper into their individual stories, the levels start to click, and the mechanics stop feeling random and begin to feel purposeful.

By the end, it made me tear twice, so it clearly succeeds at developing its characters. That said, the main narrative never stops feeling extremely dumb.

I guess my main complaint is that It Takes Two tightly integrated story and gameplay, while Split Fiction often felt like a sequence of creative ideas stitched together without a strong backbone. 

Still, as a co-op experience, it’s a blast. Constantly changing mechanics keep it fresh, even if that same design sometimes works against the overall cohesion.

If they keep pushing this formula further though, the cow will run out of milk fast.

Fantastic work Hazelight. Bravo!", 'ww', 'Excellent fun two player game. Just the right balance of challenge without being frustratingly difficult.', 'Split Fiction is the absolute peak of Hazelight. They went all in, completely all in.

name: PlanetSide 2
categories: ['Multi-player', 'MMO', 'PvP', 'Online PvP', 'Steam Trading Cards', 'Steam Cloud']
short_description: PlanetSide 2 is an all-out planetary war, where thousands of players battle as one across enormous continents. Utilize infantry, ground and air vehicles, and teamwork to destroy your enemies in this revolutionary first-person shooter on a massive scale.
detailed_description_raw:
PlanetSide 2 is a free-to-play, massively multiplayer online first person shooter (MMOFPS).

Empires and their soldiers battle in an all-out planetary war on a scale never before seen, in stunning, breathtaking detail. PlanetSide 2 battles persist and the war never ends, offering constant challenges of individual skill, team grit, and empire-wide coordination. Take up arms and drop into intense infantry, vehicle, and air combat.

Players come together in enormous battles across four massive continents to win control of critical territories, gaining key resources for their empire. With an extensive skill tree and class-based system, players can customize their soldier, weapons, and vehicles to match their playstyle and meet the needs of their squads, outfits, and empires. In the world of PlanetSide 2, every soldier makes a difference.

THREE WARRING EMPIRES

Players will choose to align with one of three empires: the militaristic, authoritarian Terran Republic; the rebellious, freedom-fighting New Conglomerate; or the technocratic, alien-influenced Vanu Sovereignty. Each empire has access to empire-specific weapons, attachments, vehicles, abilities, and more.


MASSIVE WARFARE

Battles take place not between dozens of soldiers, but between hundreds. They fight on foot. They pile into vehicles. They take to the skies in devastating aircraft. Each battleground holds valuable resources and strategic positions, and the empire that can conquer and hold these territories will be rewarded with the resources and the means to achieve victory.

ENORMOUS MAPS

PlanetSide 2 features four incredible and diverse continent maps with dozens of square kilometers of seamless gameplay space, every inch of which is hand-crafted and contestable. Whether in open fields, barren desert, in armed and armored bases, or in the skies, victory will rely on knowing your surroundings.


PERSISTENCE THAT PAYS

In PlanetSide 2 the war isn’t won by a single base capture. The core gameplay of PlanetSide 2 is about holding crucial territories and controlling resources. Working strategically as a team to secure tactical positions has long-lasting effects that can shift the tide of battle.

CLASS-BASED COMBAT

Players can build their soldier to match wants and their allies' needs. Six distinct classes provide a wealth of squad options and combat tactics. Grow your soldier over time as you master each combat role, weapon, and vehicle, laterally unlocking hundreds of weapons, attachments, gear, skills, vehicles, and more.




Heavy Assault: Rush into the battle guns blazing. You are the dedicated foot soldier of Auraxis.

Light Assault: Go where the Heavies can’t with your short-burst jetpack. Pick a high spot, throw a grenade, and out maneuver your foes!

Combat Medic: Keep your fellow soldiers alive and in fighting shape. You are the beating heart of any squad.

Infiltrator: Stay silent. Stay invisible. You are death from the shadows, whether with a knife from behind or a single shot from a sniper's nest. You are the enemy's constant fear.

Engineer: Deploy crucial equipment. Resupply your allies. Fix the thing, then fix it again. You keep the machines running, the tanks firing, and the war effort moving forward.

MAX: Step into your Mechanized Assault Exo-Suit (MAX). Cannons for hands, armor for flesh, and a disposition to match, you are a walking mass of nigh unstoppable death.

OUTFIT TEAMWORK

Join or form your own Outfit, a like-minded group of soldiers who train together day in and day out. Whether a small rapid response team or a massive clan, Outfits are vital to each empire's strategic organization.


VEHICLES & WEAPONS

Train and equip your soldier how you want, with a huge array of weapons and vehicles which can be extensively customized by preference or purpose, using attachments, upgrades, and add-ons earned in the war.

PLAY FOR FREE

Jump up, kick back, whip around and spin!', 'awesome!', 'I played this game for about 4.1 hours and finished the main story. No 100%, no side content—just a casual playthrough to see what it’s like after picking it up on a heavy discount.

Story

The story is basically just the movie. And I mean literally.

Instead of properly adapting it, the game uses actual movie footage mixed with in-game cutscenes, and the transitions between them are really bad. It feels disjointed and awkward, like it cuts in the middle of scenes and then jumps into gameplay.

Because of that, the story feels pointless. There’s no real reason for this to exist as a game. It would have been much better if they created an original story instead of just copying the movie.

Gameplay & Combat

This is easily the best part of the game.

The combat system is the best I’ve seen in a LEGO game so far
Combos are fun and more engaging than usual
It actually feels like they tried to evolve the gameplay

However:

There is noticeable input delay / responsiveness issues
The game feels restricted early on because you don’t have full abilities
It takes almost the entire game (around 90–95%) to unlock everything, which hurts the pacing badly

The puzzles are decent, but limited early on due to lack of abilities. Once everything opens up, it gets better—but by then, you’re basically done with the game.

Characters

The characters are exactly what you’d expect from the movie/show versions.

They’re fine, but nothing particularly special in this game
Lord Garmadon stands out the most
Kai is good here, but compared to the original show, he feels less like a strong leader and more toned down

The movie versions and show versions are slightly different, and personally, I prefer the show’s characterization more.

Level Design

This is another strong point.

Environments look great
Levels are visually interesting
Fun to move through, even if you’re not exploring deeply

I didn’t spend much time exploring, but from what I saw, the world design itself is solid.

Humor & Tone

The humor is actually decent compared to other LEGO games.

Not laugh-out-loud funny
But definitely better than some other entries
Works well for kids especially
Technical
No crashes or major bugs
Graphics are good for a 2017 LEGO game
Sound design is solid

Main issue:

Noticeable input delay during gameplay, especially in combat
Final Thoughts

I didn’t really enjoy finishing this game.

If I went back in time, I probably wouldn’t buy it again—even on sale. The gameplay shows real potential, especially the combat system, but everything else feels like a lazy movie tie-in.

Recommendation
If you’re a Ninjago fan: You might enjoy it
If you just like LEGO games: Only get it on a deep sale
Otherwise: It’s very skippable

short_description: Paradox Development Studio invites you to build your ideal society in the tumult of the exciting and transformative 19th century. Balance the competing interests in your society and earn your place in the sun in Victoria 3, one of the most anticipated games in Paradox’s history.
detailed_description_raw:
Roadmap


About the Game

Paradox Development Studio invites you to build your ideal society in the tumult of the exciting and transformative 19th century. Balance the competing interests in your society and earn your place in the sun in Victoria 3, one of the most anticipated games in Paradox’s history.

  • Lead dozens of world nations from 1836-1936. Agrarian or Industrial, Traditional or Radical, Peaceful or Expansionist... the choice is yours.

  • Detailed population groups with their own economic needs and political desires.

  • Reform your government and constitution to take advantage of new social innovations, or preserve the stability of your nation by holding fast to tradition in the face of revolutionaries.

  • Research transformative new technology or ideas to improve your national situation.

  • Expand your industry to take advantage of lucrative goods, taxing the profits to improve national prosperity.

  • Import cheap raw materials to cover your basic needs while finding new markets for your finished goods.

  • Secure vital goods to fuel your advanced economy and control the fate of empires.

  • Balance employing available labor force with the needs for new types of workers.

  • Use your diplomatic wiles to weave a tangled global web of pacts, relations, alliances, and rivalries to secure your diplomatic position on the world stage.

  • Employ threats, military prowess and bluffs to persuade enemies to back down in conflicts.

  • Increase your economic and military strength at the expense of rivals.

  • Accumulate prestige and the respect of your rivals as you build an industrial giant at home or an empire abroad.

short_description: Hunt, survive, build and lead in the harsh Middle Ages: Create your own Medieval Dynasty and ensure its long-lasting prosperity or die trying! Play alone or team up with friends to enjoy the ultimate medieval experience.
detailed_description_raw:
Roadmap


About the Game

Find a piece of land where you could build your home. You start as a simple farmer or a hunter, but after you gain more experience, learn and hone your skills, you can become the head of a prospering town. Design, build and improve your house - from a simple hut to an extensive farm. Convince people to join your village. Find your true love and create a family, ensuring the continuity of your dynasty. Play solo or team up with friends and explore the beautiful but harsh medieval world in the Co-Op Mode together.

An expansive, open world awaits you. Face hot summer months and cold winters and make sure that neither you nor your people lack anything.

Medieval Dynasty will captivate you with its unique genre combination for the ultimate medieval experience!

Defy the seasons: Make sure that neither you nor your population starve or die of thirst. Hunt wild animals, plant crops or trade resources to be ready for anything.

It is in your hands: Without the right tools, you won't get far – whether you're hunting, chopping trees, harvesting or building. But watch out for wear and tear so that your pickaxe doesn't become unusable in the middle of nowhere.

Live and learn: Develop your character, specialize, or become a true jack-of-all-trades as you interact with NPCs, care for your family, explore the lush world, or complete tasks for others.

Start a village: You are a town planner, builder and warden all in one! Coordinate the layout, manage the residents, gather resources, produce goods and trade to help the community grow and prosper.

The medieval world, with its dangers and gorgeous surroundings is in your reach!

  • Realistic Open World: Explore two expansive maps, the Valley (Solo) and The Oxbow (Solo and Co-Op) in a beautiful open world.

  • Cooperative Multiplayer: Team up with up to 4 friends for a cooperative gaming experience.

  • Character Customization: Personalize your avatar with a detailed character creator.

  • Dynamic Environmental Realism: Immerse yourself in a complete 4-season cycle with dynamic day/night transitions and realistic weather, all while actively engaging with the environment, from felling trees to exploring caves.

  • Structural and Interior Diversity: Create a diverse range of over 25 buildings, from humble shacks to sturdy stone houses, and personalize them with a selection of over 120 furniture and decoration pieces.

  • Extensive Crafting: Craft 300+ pieces, including weapons, tools, furniture, and clothing.

  • Wildlife Interaction: Encounter wolves, boars, deer, wisent and more wild animals populating the forests.

  • Survival Mechanics: Monitor your food, water, health, and stamina levels.

  • Skill Tree: Develop your character by buying new skills.

  • Unique Events: Experience dynamic events and decisions with direct gameplay impact.

  • Quests and Economy: Engage in quests, trade, and economic activities.

  • Social Dynamics and Family Building: Create connections with NPCs, establish your own family, and develop relationships that may lead to the joy of parenthood in the game and provide you the opportunity to pass your legacy on to the next generation.

  • Interactive environment: You'll be captivated by the expansive landscape that dynamically changes with your actions. Cut down trees, collect resources or explore mysterious caves: Everywhere you’ll find something new to discover.
store_description: About This Game Find a piece of land where you could build your home. You start as a simple farmer or a hunter, but after you gain more experience, learn and hone your skills, you can become the head of a prospering town. Design, build and improve your house - from a simple hut to an extensive farm. Convince people to join your village. Find your true love and create a family, ensuring the continuity of your dynasty. Play solo or team up with friends and explore the beautiful but harsh medieval world in the Co-Op Mode together. An expansive, open world awaits you. Face hot summer months and cold winters and make sure that neither you nor your people lack anything. Medieval Dynasty will captivate you with its unique genre combination for the ultimate medieval experience! Defy the seasons: Make sure that neither you nor your population starve or die of thirst. Hunt wild animals, plant crops or trade resources to be ready for anything. It is in your hands: Without the right tools, you won't get far – whether you're hunting, chopping trees, harvesting or building. But watch out for wear and tear so that your pickaxe doesn't become unusable in the middle of nowhere. Live and learn: Develop your character, specialize, or become a true jack-of-all-trades as you interact with NPCs, care for your family, explore the lush world, or complete tasks for others. Start a village: You are a town planner, builder and warden all in one! Coordinate the layout, manage the residents, gather resources, produce goods and trade to help the community grow and prosper. The medieval world, with its dangers and gorgeous surroundings is in your reach! Realistic Open World: Explore two expansive maps, the Valley (Solo) and The Oxbow (Solo and Co-Op) in a beautiful open world. Cooperative Multiplayer: Team up with up to 4 friends for a cooperative gaming experience. Character Customization: Personalize your avatar with a detailed character creator. D ynamic Environmental Realism: Immerse yourself in a complete 4-season cycle with dynamic day/night transitions and realistic weather, all while actively engaging with the environment, from felling trees to exploring caves. Structural and Interior Diversity: Create a diverse range of over 25 buildings, from humble shacks to sturdy stone houses, and personalize them with a selection of over 120 furniture and decoration pieces. Extensive Crafting: Craft 300+ pieces, including weapons, tools, furniture, and clothing. Wildlife Interaction: Encounter wolves, boars, deer, wisent and more wild animals populating the forests. Survival Mechanics: Monitor your food, water, health, and stamina levels. Skill Tree: Develop your character by buying new skills. Unique Events: Experience dynamic events and decisions with direct gameplay impact. Quests and Economy: Engage in quests, trade, and economic activities. Social Dynamics and Family Building: Create connections with NPCs, establish your own family, and develop relationships that may lead to the joy of parenthood in the game and provide you the opportunity to pass your legacy on to the next generation. Interactive environment: You'll be captivated by the expansive landscape that dynamically changes with your actions. Cut down trees, collect resources or explore mysterious caves: Everywhere you’ll find something new to discover.

['', 'Just cause you had part in Wild West Dinasty I will give you a ♥♥♥♥♥♥ review,you published that game,never finished it,allowing studio to use your name to sell the game,you split the profit with them for sold games,then you people just changed name of a game before launching it on consoles,still in unfinished state,still crashing and being buggy as pre alpha type of a game,you made this awesome game but then you got greedy and started cheating people sending out games that in 2 years had 5 updates all together like Wild West,and you call it 1.0???based on what?that is worst rated game on steam i have seen so far,and you had a part of it and robbing us for it...do something good when you put your name on line ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥,not just to steal our money based on 1 good game that you made..and why should anyone pay for game guide that comes free with almost all games ever invented?I am supposed to pay for something to teach me how to play a game that I already paid?did you lost your mind or just your soul?', "The game is absoloutley amazingh and grindy eben in 2026. Such a fun game to play and honestly one of it's kind.", 'Everything would be perfect if it weren’t for one developer mistake.
WHY can I steal everything but NOT a horse???
I want to build a real medieval Romani community! Also, I don’t want to spend so much time running from one village to another.
I WANT TO STEAL A HORSE. PLEASE FIX THIS.

Thank you, the game is good btw.', "Buy this! It's a decent game, Thank you devs!", 'Bought by accident (mistook for another title). Realized exactly 2 weeks later, never even started the game. Apparently too late for glorious Valve for a refund. Now a poor person being even poorer for no reason. Thanks Gaben...', 'good', 'great game, i love it. i would like to see either and update to the economy or maybe setting where you can tweak the economy yourself. currently basic recipes, meaning items that are crafted from only 1-2 ingredients outshine most of the more complex recipes that require 3+ ingredients in terms of profit. i still recommend the game to anyone that loves colony sims, especially ones that let you get your hands dirty by being a person in the village as well instead of the usual top-down invisible entity that manages the colony', 'This is where I want to retire', "I love this game, don't get me wrong playing solo building takes FOREVER, but in co-op it's just perfect. My husband and I play this and tend to just play it all day and nearly all night for days at a time and never get bored with it. The graphics are beautiful. I'm excited to see where this game goes as they release more updates.", 'Obsessed', 'One of the games I never delete from my library. For base building enthusiasts, this will check all the boxes! Still playing after so many hours.', 'Love how it feels like you're in the time periods with having accurate fashion (I know for sure on the one dress at least lol), hairstyles and it seems also the names - so as a history nerd I really enjoy that aspect. I have yet to encournter any real upsets with the game at all, though my personal upsets are about being unable to furnish the homes (more than a few dangling items or fur rugs) and that as the player character being unable to have more than one child - both of which are likely unique to me lol I wish there was also the ability to type in a name for your heir but that's all just really me wanting the cake and to eat it too. 

The only frustrating thing I ever came across is the need to have level terrain to do any building. The game makes it seem like you're able to build anywhere in the map not already taken by another village - and technically you *can* do that...however if the land is not level then you can't do much at all. Which seems to limit the place you can actually build a village strategically. So to those starting the game or looking to play it, keep in mind to pick the flattest areas you can find if you don't want to be constantly fanaggling or having distance between your village buildings. 

All in all though, it's a really nice and relaxing game that is pretty immersive (although while I like to blast 80s and 90s music while playing to live my best Knight's Tale life, i think playing more folk and fantasy music would better suck you into your gaming) and allows you to live your renfaire life with ease.

P.s. 
Find it so ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ funny to see my husband being labelled as my "wife" because it was easier to bring in the ability to make the player be able to be a girl lmao (No, it seriously cracks me up)', "Had to return.  People have complained about the font scale literally for years and the devs refuse to patch it and there's no mod support.", 'Played in SIngleplayer

When i first saw the skill system i was expecting something akin to Valheim, get better at the things you do often, instead, you get a semi-generic perk point relevant to the thing you were doing. Like Skyrim but instead of having 18 skill trees entirely specific to the activity, you have 6 trees that are generally relevant. Gathering can level survival, which lets you buff up your poison resistance or axe condition...

Too much time investment is required for the basic buildings. about 80 total items of logs, sticks & 2 other things for the most basic house, which is your first shelter.

I didn't see a campfire in the quick build menu but the game references it in the tutorial.

The world felt empty, trees everywhere, a few villages sprinkled around but looking around just felt dull.

The NPCs had external monologues often but i don't think i heard the villagers actually communicate to each other. NPCs walk & stand like generic bots.

"Have you heard anything interesting" -> "I heard someone in X village requires your help" - WTF is that response. There was no deviation, i asked several NPCs & they all said the same thing. No tips about the wilderness, animal behaviours, genuine good advice or warnings, just a quest direction for a village 20 Minutes away that somehow needs MY help specifically.

Talking to the toddlers gives a funny "Mama told me not to talk to strangers" dialogue, why does a 4-year old look like a 1-year old who just learned to walk...', 'This game is what ive been looking for, for years, its great', 'For those who love the challenge of building a village, combined with the flare of medieval imagery, you will love this game. The beginning storyline is short, but robust, however, the developers have injected more into the game as they continue to update the game. You can hunt game, kill bandits, and develop an intricate village into a small town, and even larger. You can decorate the houses and the landscape of your village in many ways, making Medieval Dynasty a game that you can come back to time after time. 

With the introduction of Oxbow, you can even create your own character, male or female, and even play with others to grow a village that reflects their style as well as your own. Whether you play as Racimir in the original valley, or as your own creation in the valley of Oxbow, you will be faced with challenges that keep you immersed in the game for many hours. I originally played this game on XBox, but now play on PC and I loved it so much that it was well worth paying for again. I hope that you will experience the same fascination for this game as I have, and who knows, maybe one day I will meet you in Oxbow. Now, go and build the greatest village that has ever been seen by anyone!', 'it is good. a good game', 'Very chill, nice attention to detail. Great if you just want to have a relaxing build and management experience. Villager management is fairly complex and involved, can take some getting used to.', 'Enjoyed the game the customisation but once I build all the buildings, felt like the game ended but would recommend and likely will play the other map.', 'A worthy if not slightly clumsy simulation with an odd obsession with money/gold. There's plenty of hunting, farming, fishing, and building to keep the player occupied for hours. Quest chains are also available for those that want a more directed experience. The sandbox appeal is where it's at for me.  
I'd prefer a more customized approach to building rather than the pre-made structures available, but that doesn't take away from the overall experience. Expect a few bugs here and there (this is the first game I've ever played with an 'unstuck' command right there on the game menu). The bouncing fallen trees and the NPCs sitting on air are amusing. The self-aware peasants add a certain flair and tongue-in-cheek atmosphere. There are plenty of easter egg allusions! I've noticed references/homages/quotes from everything from Star Wars to Stanger Things to Waiting for Godot, and even Half Life 2 and the Steve Miller Band. I especially like it when they say "Winter is coming."  Expect to read a lot of text.  
Just about the only negative aspect is the strange obsession with gold/money. Buying technology with gold feels a little strange considering that the player also has to pay taxes with the same gold. This leads to a strange stagflation in which the player cannot use produce or cattle to compensate the King instead of using gold. This in turn compels the player to craft items to simply take as much gold out of the local settlements as possible in order to just break even during tax season. Playing Richie Rich in a medieval simulator can be fun, if not a little silly. Luckily the player can sell virtually any item to any vendor and the inventory of each vendor resets after each season passes, so this isn't too difficult or frustrating, but it feels a little weird having to spend money just to learn how to craft and/or build something. 
The passing of the seasons and how it affects farming is a major strength here that gives a slim but reasonable amount of realism. Parents will note that the sex and violence is minimal. The player seems to only be allowed to reproduce once after the courtship meter has maxed out. There is no nudity or anything like that, just a mate who will "rock your world" and then wake up the next day pregnant. Hearing the mother's coo their babies and the peasants complain about the seasons, hangovers, and the tedium of daily medieval life is most welcome. Watch out for the aggressive wolves! I especially like to hunt where the wolves chase the moose.', "I'm kind of surprised I have never reviewed this game! I've played the hell out of it numerous times and always find it to be more interesting than the last!", 'so good', 'This game is one of the best of its kind that I have ever played. It is fairly complicated and quite hard to balance, especially at the beginning (I refuse to read strategy or bets practice guides, as I want to experience the game myself and am not interested in pre-chewed tips by others). 

A candidate for GOTY in my opinion. Devs love their game, update periodically and try to do their best listening to the community, released only one DLC so far which is fairly priced etc. 

If you like medieval settings, management games and survival elements, this one is a must play.', "This game started as a stray experiment as I looked around for something that could scratch that 3D city/building/managing sim itch. However, it blossomed into so much more when I started playing with my autistic daughter, who at 17, took to this game right away. It is our go-to relax game that feeds us things to do while we converse away on Discord. I absolutely love this game for having brought my daughter and I closer. She opens up to me in ways she never did before simply while we're cutting down forests of trees or cooking up a new batch of potage. It's amazing!", 'This is one of the best games I have played in decades. A perfect blend in my opinion of the city-builders, farming sims, and cozy crafting games mixed in with a little bit of monster (animals) hunting and open-world quests. I love this game so much that I own it on multiple platforms and I would happily purchase it again and again for any of my friends who wanted to play with or without me.', "Game is ok. I had hoped it was multiplyer Skyrim. Instead, its just a sandbox game medieval flavor. You build anywhere, get materials, build things, recruit NPCs, and repeat. The quest are not that interesting and are timed to the season, which is only a few days unless you change that in the settings. Interactions are kinda clunky and animation is not exactly smooth. Could be a fun game. I'm just not dedicated to it right now.", '<3', "The game is really interesting, although I'm currently playing in single-player mode. The possibility of playing with others makes it more exciting, and I'd like to see what it's like in multiplayer in the future. The challenges are real, in that they allow a single player to progress through the story, develop their skills, and complete objectives. On the other hand, my experience is currently limited, so I can't elaborate much more than what I've already said, but it's definitely a game worth playing for anyone who enjoys open-world games that allow you to explore, build, fight, and survive (in all its aspects).", 'Awesome game']

short_description: Ushering in a new era for the METAL GEAR franchise with cutting-edge technology powered by the Fox Engine, METAL GEAR SOLID V: The Phantom Pain, will provide players a first-rate gaming experience as they are offered tactical freedom to carry out open-world missions.
detailed_description_raw:
Konami Digital Entertainment continues forth the ‘METAL GEAR SOLID V Experience’ with the latest chapter, METAL GEAR SOLID V: The Phantom Pain. Ushering in a new era for the franchise with cutting-edge technology powered by the Fox Engine, MGSV: The Phantom Pain will provide players a first-rate gaming experience as they are offered tactical freedom to carry out open-world missions.

Nine years after the events of MGSV: GROUND ZEROES and the fall of Mother Base, Snake a.k.a. Big Boss, awakens from a nine year coma. The year is 1984. The Cold War serves as the backdrop as nuclear weapons continue to shape a global crisis. Driven by revenge, Snake establishes a new private army and returns to the battlefield in pursuit of the shadow group, XOF.

The METAL GEAR SOLID team continues to ambitiously explore mature themes such as the psychology of warfare and the atrocities that result from those that engage in its vicious cycle. One of the most anticipated games of the year with its open-world design, photorealistic visual fidelity and feature-rich game design, MGSV: The Phantom Pain will leave its mark as one of the hallmarks in the gaming industry for its cinematic storytelling, heavy themes, and immersive tactical gameplay.

Key Features:
- Open-World game design allowing players ultimate freedom on how to approach missions and overall game progression
- Fox Engine delivers photorealistic graphics, thoughtful game design and true new-generation game production quality
- Online connectivity that carries the experience beyond the consoles to other devices to augment the overall functionality and access to the game.

["When I first played this I was a bit overwhelmed with the open world-ish approach they had, but I ended up warming up to it pretty quick. I think every has the same opinion about the story of MGSV so I won't echo it here. I loved the gameplay in this so much. I'm not one to stick around much after finishing a game's story, but this got me hooked and made me want to get all achievements (which I just finished today). I kinda feel sad since I have to put this game down now after that, but I totally see myself coming back for FOB missions from time to time.", 'Definetly the best game i ever played, it can make a grown man cry!!', "I think it's time to review 2026/04/23. After a long time of grinding and monthly intermittent work on gear development, I have accomplished a lot. In conclusion, this game is something more than the stealth genre and nothing is going to be like this, it's one of a kind.
You have a private militia. I mostly go through the CV application of their stats by using scopes. Getting them extracted is fun. You can go non-lethal, knock them out, and sneak away. Every base or location has some sort of climbable surface or hole you can get through. You can use sleep or electrocution gadgets, and also a box to slowly blend in with the environment, as well as distract guards or use a decoy. You mostly use the non-lethal sleep dart pistol. I found the Burkov in later development to be better. I mostly use the air shotgun with low silencer durability and a non-lethal sniper. If I am against a heavy unit, fulton extraction is usually my option when going non-lethal. For kill missions, you can use a downed enemy as a weapon to finish them off. which mgsv is always fun to me. because of the possiblity in the open world is just cool.", 'dude........ ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ yes...', 'Mega Gay Sawcon V: The Fanum Tax', 'Don`t waste your money.
buy mgr or mgs 1,2,3 and the new 4 if its well ported 
', "it's by kojima just buy it you already know its good", "One of the best, if not the best stealth game of all time. The game gives you infinite ways to infiltrate bases. You can go full stealthy, kill everyone, snipe the target from far away, call an airstrike, use many interesting items or even drive up with a tank and blow everything up. Even though it's a long game, it still has good replayability due to the amazing gameplay and amount of freedom you have.

The enemy's AI is incredible. They adapt to your playstyle so you have to change up your tactics from time to time.

This game looks amazing and is very optimized thanks to the Fox Engine, it can easily run on very old hardware. It's sad to see the Fox engine be ditched just like that.

But the game is not all perfect, it has its flaws. The open world does feel empty sometimes and I wish there were bigger bases like Camp Omega from Ground Zeroes. The story starts very nicely but as you go it goes downhill. The ending feels rushed and unfinished. 
Most boss fights feel pretty bad compared to the other ones in the series. 

Overall this is definitely one of my favorite games of all time and I always come back to replay.", 'Hideo Kojima', 'darker then mgs delta (the only other mgs game i have played so far). story is even more crazy then delta and feel like a bit closer to death stranding at times. gameplay is really good. it takes supriceingly small in disk space given the amount of stuff in the game and the price is very worth it. 9/10 game', 'A Masterpiece.', 'There is a Spider man scene here', 'Cool stealth game.', 'I have over 3000 hours on PS4 but I wanted to switch off of consoles as a whole, so I bought it on here. short_description: Assetto Corsa v1.16 introduces the new "Laguna Seca" laser-scanned track, 7 new cars among which the eagerly awaited Alfa Romeo Giulia Quadrifoglio! Check the changelog for further info!
detailed_description_raw:
NEXT GENERATION RACING SIMULATOR
Assetto Corsa features an advanced DirectX 11 graphics engine that recreates an immersive environment, dynamic lighthing and realistic materials and surfaces. The advanced physics engine is being designed to provide a very realistic driving experience, including features and aspects of real cars, never seen on any other racing simulator such as tyre flat spots, heat cycles including graining and blistering, very advanced aerodynamic simulation with active movable aerodynamics parts controlled in real time by telemetry input channels, hybrid systems with kers and energy recovery simulation. Extremely detailed with single player and multiplayer options, exclusive licensed cars reproduced with the best accuracy possible, thanks to the official cooperation of Car Manufacturers.
ASSETTO CORSA has been developed at the KUNOS Simulazioni R&D office, located just inside the international racing circuit of Vallelunga, allowing the team to develop the game with the cooperation of real world racing drivers and racing teams.

LEGENDARY TRACKS
The circuits have been developed using Laser scan technology, in order to guarantee the highest level of accuracy possible in reproducing real world motor racing environments.
Monza, Silverstone, Imola, Mugello, Spa, Brands Hatch and many more tracks, including the historical reproduction of Monza, that brings again to life the legendary Sopraelevata, the high-speed oval where races were staged until 1961.

EXCLUSIVE CARS
The Assetto Corsa physics engine is all new, using practical knowledge acquired from working closely with the elite of motorsport in order to engineer the best possible accuracy of physics and tactility of feeling.
A physics engine like this begs to be used with officially licensed contents: Abarth, Audi, BMW, Classic Team Lotus, Ferrari, KTM, Lamborghini, Lotus cars, McLaren, Mercedes, Scuderia Glickenhaus, Pagani, Porsche, Tatuus and many more!

HARDWARE COMPATIBILITY
Enjoy ASSETTO CORSA using any kind of game device: keyboard, joypad, joystick, steering wheels including any kind of professional device and the most common motion systems.
ASSETTO CORSA is also compatible and ready for Oculus and OpenVR/VIVE, triple monitor view, Track IR and 3D VISION.

GAMEPLAY & FEATURES
Assetto Corsa includes a career mode, a list of special and unique events and challenges, as well as a fully customizable, single player and multiplayer modes featuring quick races, custom championships, race weekends including free practice session, qualifying session and race. Drag races, drift challenges and much more! Four driving assist profiles (gamer, racer, pro, plus a fully-customizable profile) allow any kind of player to enjoy the simulation at their desired level.
Advanced setup options and telemetry for data analysis; dynamic simulation of the tyre rubber deposited on track, depending on the car laps; an adjustable time of the day mode, featuring sun position calculated in realtime, depending by geographical coordinates of the track and by the sun curve according to time and date, in order to get the same light conditions of the real tracks!

MODDING & CUSTOMIZATION
Assetto Corsa will allow for considerable customisation and modification, in order to satisfy the expectations of professional simracers, gamers who prefer to approach the driving experience more progressively, and hobbyists who just like to reproduce and share their cars and tracks, taking advantage of the same editing tools developed and used by the developers of the game.

store_description: About This Game NEXT GENERATION RACING SIMULATOR Assetto Corsa features an advanced DirectX 11 graphics engine that recreates an immersive environment, dynamic lighthing and realistic materials and surfaces. The advanced physics engine is being designed to provide a very realistic driving experience, including features and aspects of real cars, never seen on any other racing simulator such as tyre flat spots, heat cycles including graining and blistering, very advanced aerodynamic simulation with active movable aerodynamics parts controlled in real time by telemetry input channels, hybrid systems with kers and energy recovery simulation. Extremely detailed with single player and multiplayer options, exclusive licensed cars reproduced with the best accuracy possible, thanks to the official cooperation of Car Manufacturers. ASSETTO CORSA has been developed at the KUNOS Simulazioni R&D office, located just inside the international racing circuit of Vallelunga, allowing the team to develop the game with the cooperation of real world racing drivers and racing teams. LEGENDARY TRACKS The circuits have been developed using Laser scan technology, in order to guarantee the highest level of accuracy possible in reproducing real world motor racing environments. Monza, Silverstone, Imola, Mugello, Spa, Brands Hatch and many more tracks, including the historical reproduction of Monza, that brings again to life the legendary Sopraelevata, the high-speed oval where races were staged until 1961. EXCLUSIVE CARS The Assetto Corsa physics engine is all new, using practical knowledge acquired from working closely with the elite of motorsport in order to engineer the best possible accuracy of physics and tactility of feeling. A physics engine like this begs to be used with officially licensed contents: Abarth, Audi, BMW, Classic Team Lotus, Ferrari, KTM, Lamborghini, Lotus cars, McLaren, Mercedes, Scuderia Glickenhaus, Pagani, Porsche, Tatuus and many more! HARDWARE COMPATIBILITY Enjoy ASSETTO CORSA using any kind of game device: keyboard, joypad, joystick, steering wheels including any kind of professional device and the most common motion systems. ASSETTO CORSA is also compatible and ready for Oculus and OpenVR/VIVE, triple monitor view, Track IR and 3D VISION. GAMEPLAY & FEATURES Assetto Corsa includes a career mode, a list of special and unique events and challenges, as well as a fully customizable, single player and multiplayer modes featuring quick races, custom championships, race weekends including free practice session, qualifying session and race. Drag races, drift challenges and much more! Four driving assist profiles (gamer, racer, pro, plus a fully-customizable profile) allow any kind of player to enjoy the simulation at their desired level. Advanced setup options and telemetry for data analysis; dynamic simulation of the tyre rubber deposited on track, depending on the car laps; an adjustable time of the day mode, featuring sun position calculated in realtime, depending by geographical coordinates of the track and by the sun curve according to time and date, in order to get the same light conditions of the real tracks! MODDING & CUSTOMIZATION Assetto Corsa will allow for considerable customisation and modification, in order to satisfy the expectations of professional simracers, gamers who prefer to approach the driving experience more progressively, and hobbyists who just like to reproduce and share their cars and tracks, taking advantage of the same editing tools developed and used by the developers of the game. short_description: As Lara Croft races to save the world from a Maya apocalypse, she must become the Tomb Raider she is destined to be.
detailed_description_raw:
FREE TRIAL


Reviews & Accolades

“The strongest entry in the trilogy” - GamesRadar+
“Arrestingly Beautiful” - Polygon
“Crisp, addictive, and deeply satisfying” - Nerdist
“Looks absolutely incredible” - Destructoid
“A truly immersive experience.” - Bleacher Report
“9 out of 10” - Worthplaying
“Exploration is absolutely vibrant” - GameCrate
“A tremendous thrill ride” - Digital Trends
“9 out of 10 - Better than ever before." - Outright Geekery


Shadow of the Tomb Raider Definitive Edition out now for macOS and Linux.


About the Game


In Shadow of the Tomb Raider Definitive Edition experience the final chapter of Lara’s origin as she is forged into the Tomb Raider she is destined to be. Combining the base game, all seven DLC challenge tombs, as well as all downloadable weapons, outfits, and skills, Shadow of the Tomb Raider Definitive Edition is the ultimate way to experience Lara’s defining moment.

Survive and Thrive In the Deadliest Place on Earth: Master an unforgiving jungle setting in order to survive. Explore underwater environments filled with crevasses and deep tunnel systems.

Become One With the Jungle: Outgunned and outnumbered, Lara must use the jungle to her advantage. Strike suddenly and disappear like a jaguar, use mud as camouflage, and instill fear in enemies to sow chaos.

Discover Dark and Brutal Tombs: Tombs are more terrifying than ever before, requiring advanced traversal techniques to reach them, and once inside they are filled with deadly puzzles.

Uncover Living History: Discover a hidden city and explore the biggest hub space ever found in a Tomb Raider game.
developer: Eidos-Montréal, Crystal Dynamics, Nixxes, Feral Interactive (Mac), Feral interactive (Linux)
publisher: Crystal Dynamics, Feral Interactive (Mac), Feral interactive (Linux)
